He signaled to the bartender for two firewhiskys, post haste. ÊShe is the best thing that ever happened to me and Merlin knows what I did to deserve this,Ë Theo continued and Dracoıs brain screamed Oh, me too, mate, youıve no idea, but now that the flood gates had opened, he figured it best to let Theo get everything out. Theo suddenly looked panicked and shook his head. ÊI love her but she doesnıt know, she has no idea and how the hell do I fix this?Ë ÊWait, Iım confused. I thought you said this woman wants to move in with you? She doesnıt know you love her?Ë Theo still wouldnıt look him in the eye, just stared down into his lager dejectedly. ÊYou wouldnıt understand. Youıll never understand.Ë ÊHow about you fucking try me instead of assuming, yeah?Ë Draco burst out angrily. After all the shitty family drama theyıd had to endure in their young lives, did Theo really think Draco couldnıt handle whatever romantic nonsense he was all broken up over? Theo shook his head and released a pained sigh. ÊI love herÐË ÊYeah, I got that bit.Ë ÊI love her and she doesnıt know that Iım╔ that Iım a╔Ë Theo drained his glass again. ÊShe doesnıt know Iım a wizard.Ë Dracoıs mind tried to work furiously through what Theo had just said because it did not make any sense. ÊHow?Ë Was all Draco managed. ÊBecause I havenıt told her what I am. Because I donıt even know how to approach this. Because╔ sheıs a Muggle.Ë The bartender arrived then with a bottle of Ogdenıs and two glasses. ÊLeave the bottle,Ë Draco ordered and the man shrugged and walked off. Draco filled the two glasses to the brim and pushed one towards Theo. ÊHow did this happen?Ë He asked in a hushed voice. Theo tossed back the whisky. ÊAbout a year and a half ago I found myself in a Muggle pub╔Ë The double life of Theodore Nott Muggle London was bustling at this hour, but this version of London seemed bustling at every hour. It was frenetic, chaotic, and there were throngs of people everywhere, constantly. Theo sat on his usual bench across the way from a strip of popular pubs, eateries, and shops, summoning the courage he possessed. Heıd been coming to this exact spot every day for the last week, observing his surroundings. This field trip was the culmination of several weeks of studying and meticulous planning, of noting normal Muggle habits and behaviors. He could do this. His healer assured him he could do this. Theo needed to do this. All the burning curiosity heıd been made to keep inside his entire life had finally eked out. The books heıd found in the Ministry library from the Muggle Liaison Department had prepared him to dress and act the part, because Theo was nothing if not careful. Heıd prepped on current technologies in use, and while the first sight of motor traffic alarmed him, heıd read enough to know what to expect. They werenıt so different from the Ministry cars after all, but everything was so much louder and brighter out here. It had taken Theo this entire week for his senses to become accustomed to the busy atmosphere around him. Merlin, there were so many people, hustling to and fro, unbothered and unaware that a world of magic was concealed all around them. He finally unfurled his lanky frame from the bench and walked through the doors of the pub heıd selected as his target for this mission. He, pureblood wizard Theodore Nott, son of a prominent Death Eater, was going to have a drink in a Muggle pub and it was all going to be fine. On one of his reconnaissance visits earlier in the week, Theo had picked this particular pub based on the age of the patrons coming and going. The early evening crowd comprised young people around his age, usually in groups or couples, and it didnıt seem as rowdy as a few of the other establishments. One he wanted to avoid forever had an entire wall of glaring, overly bright boxes of light, which Theo later discovered were called televisions. Much too erratic for Theo, he wouldnıt know where to look. He took a seat on a stool at the bar of his chosen pub, and ordered a pint of Guinness. The brochure of travel tips he found in the Ministry Library titled ÊHow to Holiday in the Muggle World While Not Violating the Statute of Secrecy,Ë said this particular beverage tasted similar to a stout Theo often ordered at the Hag in the Hearth. Theo took a tentative sip and discovered the brochure description to have been accurate. Seeing as step one of this operation had gone smoothly, he relaxed a little and looked around the moderately crowded pub. This group of people were the greatest threat to the wizarding world? This unassuming bunch of humans going about their lives were somehow going to be eventually responsible for the collapse of magical society? How stupid was his father to have bought that preposterous lie? Theo quietly snorted to himself and sipped his pint as the taste steadily grew on him. Boisterous laughter suddenly burst through the air, and Theo whipped his head to the right to locate the source. And Theodore Nott was never the same again. She caught his eye immediately. Her head thrown back in glee, the unrestrained joyfulness of her laugh carrying above those of her compatriots. Her smile was wide, genuine, and her teeth glowingly white against her complexion. She swept her long black braids into a ponytail behind her head, and then caught Theoıs gaze. He looked away quickly, focusing on not spilling his pint, embarrassed at being found staring. After a few minutes, he couldnıt help himself. He looked across the room again. She was chatting animatedly with her group of friends, nodding emphatically at one of them, when she again looked up and straight at Theo. He averted his eyes. This little dance went on for several more rounds, and though Theo cursed himself every single time, he couldnıt stop his stare from wandering back to her, this vivacious woman seemed to light up the entire room. She gave him a playfully challenging grin, and Theo knew he was in trouble. Polishing off the last of his drink, he thought it was about time to settle his tab for the evening. Heıd completed what heıd set out to do, and it was time to end the charade and go back to his world. As the barkeep approached, Theo opened his mouth to speak, but an exuberant voice cut him off. ÊCan we get two more Cosmos, two lagers, an amber ale, a Guinness, and another pint of whatever this oneıs drinking?Ë She jerked her head at Theo. His mouth fell open and he tried to sputter a refusal, but she had already carried some of the drinks back to her group. She returned twice more to carry everything and still Theo could not find his voice. Finally, she returned clutching her glass of ale as the bartender slid another Guinness in front of Theo. ÊHello,Ë she said, and perched herself next to him with that wide, confident smile. ÊErm, hi. Thanks for the drink.Ë ÊWell, I got tired of waiting for you to buy me one, so I reckoned I might save you the trouble of making the first move.Ë Theo almost spat out his drink at her forwardness. ÊYou have been staring at me right? Donıt think I didnıt notice.Ë ÊIım sorry, I didnıt mean to disturb your evening,Ë he said sincerely, and she threw her head back in a loud laugh. ÊI did just buy you a drink, donıt you think if Iıd been offended I would have told you to bugger off?Ë she teased and Theo felt his entire face redden all the way to the tips of his ears. The striking woman was regarding him with mirth in her bright, amber eyes, and Theo felt a fluttering inside his chest. ÊIım Sasha,Ë she offered, and held out her hand primly. ÊNott, Theo╔ I mean╔Ë But Sasha had burst into laughter again and Theo found himself joining her this time at his blunder. ÊWell are you Theo or not?Ë she teased. ÊLet me try that again, Iım Theo Nott.Ë ÊNice to meet you Not Theo,Ë she grinned as they shook hands. Theo had never met a more easygoing person in his life. Sasha, he learned, was enrolled in the culinary school up the road, which explained the matching white jackets she and the rest of her group had slung over the backs of their chairs. He asked her question after question, eager to learn everything he could about her life. She loved culinary school, but wanted to specialize in pastry and work as a head pastry chef in a restaurant eventually. Her parents were both from Ghana and met and fell in love at university here in London. Theyıd bonded over their love of learning and were both still professors; her mother of English literature and her father of mathematics. She had two older brothers, one was a classical pianist and the other following their fatherıs footsteps in maths. Theo drunk in every word she said, fascinated, his pint of alcohol long forgotten. She eventually posed personal questions back to him, and Theo did his best to navigate around the truth. He told her his job was in finance for the government (technically true, he did work in finance but for the wizarding government). He had to wangle his backstory when it came to family, and instead told Sasha his parents were no longer around (half true; his father was dead but even though his mother remained alive and well, Theo would describe their relationship as strained, at best). She patted his arm in sympathy, her lively eyes full of sadness for him, and Theo felt a warmth spread through his body that had nothing to do with alcohol. He deftly turned the conversation back to her life, wanting to learn everything he could about her in this one night. Because when it was over, heıd go back to his lonely existence, but at least for tonight, he could be free of the burden of his heritage. Tonight he was just a bloke in a bar, chatting up a pretty girl. Her friends eventually took their leave, but Sasha waved them off with a careless hand as they passed by, seemingly too interested in spending time with Theo. He couldnıt stop the questions that tumbled forth: what was culinary school like? What was her favorite pastry to make? What was it like growing up in London? Why hadnıt she followed in her parentsı footsteps? Did she like having brothers? And on and on and on until they both jumped when the bartender announced last call. Theo felt the despair sinking in as they settled their tabs and made to leave. Heıd never felt more loose, more alive than when heıd been talking with Sasha, and now it was over and heıd never see her again. Sasha, however, had other plans. ÊHere,Ë she scribbled a series of numbers on a piece of paper and handed it to Theo. He looked down at it, confused. Was this some sort of code? And for what? ÊMy number,Ë she said with a coy smile at his perplexed look. ÊIıd love to hang out again. Maybe you could buy me the drink next time? Have a good night, Not Theo.Ë And with a cheery wave, she was off, leaving Theo standing in the middle of the sidewalk of Muggle London, clutching a scrap of paper and feeling like his entire world had shifted in but a few hours. He succumbed into a frantic madness over the next few days, the likes of which he hadnıt felt since heıd been studying for NEWTs. He needed to buy a mobile and figure out how to use it so he could communicate with Sasha. This was not like Theo, not at all. He was careful, quiet, methodical, rational. He kept his head down at the Ministry, and for the most part, no one really bothered him despite his last name. Connecting with Sasha could only lead to complications, something he studiously avoided in his current life, especially after the war. But an unexplainable urge coursed through him, an urge to seek out her company just one more time to see if those feelings from the other night had been real. So he went back to the Ministry library. He read everything he could get his hands on about Muggle communication and technology. He basically interrogated a colleague in the Muggle Liaison Department on where to buy a portable phone. He ignored the puzzled looks from a Muggleborn witch he shared an office with as he asked her to show him how to use the mobile heıd purchased. And so one week after meeting Sasha, he finally called her. Two weeks later, they went on their first date: dinner at the pub and a movie. The movie terrified Theo, (how did Muggles still have their vision and hearing? That was nothing but two hours of an assault on the senses) but he found he enjoyed sitting so close to Sasha. A week later he took her to dinner at a restaurant that had something called Ê3 Michelin starsË and Sasha seemed gobsmacked when Theo told her where theyıd be dining. Her eyes bugged at the bill, but it was nothing for Theo, and he insisted on treating her. The next week, she offered to cook for him at her flat, and Theo got his first look at a Muggle residence. He was fascinated by all the unfamiliar gadgets and appliances, but even more taken with the perfect salted caramel bread pudding she pulled out of the oven for dessert. He licked every last morsel from his spoon, and declared it the best thing heıd ever eaten. This caused the normally unerringly confident Sasha to blush with pride at the compliment and ask tentatively, ÊReally? You think itıs that good?Ë And instead of reassuring her that the dish was indeed one of the best things heıd ever tasted and that she was obscenely talented, her quiet question spurred a different reaction. ÊYouıre so beautiful,Ë he intoned softly and the moment he stopped speaking her lips were on his. The next thing he knew, she was straddling his lap in the kitchen chair as he stroked her braided hair while they kissed away the remnants of caramel from each otherıs lips and tongues. Just as Sasha went to put her hands under Theoıs shirt, the door swung open and signaled the arrival of her roommate. Theo leapt up, embarrassed at almost being seen in such a position and then at almost sending Sasha tumbling to the floor. He muttered a hurried apology and practically ran from her flat. What was he thinking? This had gone far enough. How could he have let himself get so caught up in this woman, a Muggle, when they could absolutely not have a future together? What a stupendously selfish and short-sighted bastard heıd been. These feelings of self-loathing lasted a whole week before he could no longer deny the urge to talk with her one last time. It was meant to bring closure, but the words stuck in his throat the second she picked up the phone. Instead, out tumbled an apology as he begged, begged, her to let him make it up. She was rightfully angry with him but eventually accepted his offer to take her to dinner. The restaurant was only two Michelin stars this time (so it probably wasnıt very good, reasoned Theo) but Sasha seemed impressed. However, instead of a farewell dinner, Theo laid his heart on the line. He was sorry heıd reacted poorly. He didnıt regret kissing her. He was frightened by what he felt for her. Heıd never met a woman, or even a person, like her. Heıd had a really rough childhood, his father had been a brute, and only now, after his death, did Theo finally feel free of him. Heıd never had a serious relationship before, had no clue how to act on his feelings for her. Sasha bore all of these truths stoically as he unburdened some of his darkest fears and Theo felt the knots in his chest easing with each truthful sentence he uttered. Barring Draco and his own healer, these were thoughts heıd never shared with anyone, and with each layer he peeled back he could see her eyes softening. She didnıt let him all the way off the hook that night. She thanked him for dinner and said goodbye with a chaste kiss to his cheek after heıd escorted her back to her flat. ÊYouıre a little bit broken, Not Theo, but I find I donıt mind much,Ë Sasha said softly before leaving him for the night. The next week, they met for dinner again at her flat and Theo was nervous the entire walk over. He handed her an insanely extravagant bouquet of flowers when she opened the door that made her jaw go slack. She yanked him inside by his shirt and before he could launch into a detailed explanation about each type of flower heıd selected, she crushed her lips against his and pressed him up against the door. She swiftly explained that her roommate was out for the night and pulled him along to her bedroom, the beautifully arranged flowers forgotten on the floor. A month later and he was spending every weekend at her flat. Two months later and he told her he loved her (she responded in kind). Three months in, and she invited him to meet her parents (they thought he was a bit odd and too proper at first, but his quiet demeanor, impeccable manners, and erudite observations in conversation won them over within a mere few hours of introductions). More months flew by, and as their relationship blossomed and their love grew, Theo became so immersed in Muggle society and culture that he could comfortably discuss cricket with Sashaıs father, lament global politics with her many uncles, dissect classic literature with her mother, chat about music with her pianist brother, and compare notes with her other brother on television shows and new films. It seemed the entire family was enamored with Sashaıs gangly white boyfriend with the peculiar mannerisms and quiet enthusiasm for the world around him. And every time Sasha had a question about Theoıs mysterious past, he divulged just enough of the truth without disclosing anything about his magical life. His parents were dead (oh boy did that win him sympathy points with all of her aunts) and heıd survived an abusive childhood. Heıd been sent to boarding school at a young age and had trouble fitting in, thus he didnıt have many friends to speak of. He had a steady job in finance and could afford ridiculously fine dining and a posh home as the sole heir to his deceased fatherıs fortune. Theoıs hand had been forced into buying a furnished townhome in the Muggle neighborhood of Belgravia when Sasha once remarked that they never spent time at his place. And so for the past year and a half, Theodore Nott convinced the woman he loved that he was nothing more than a Muggle accountant for the government. A lonely little rich orphan with no friends. His mother lamented that he never spent time with her anymore. His elves were restless at Nott Estate with hardly any housework to be done or orders given by their absent master. His one friend, Draco, anxious to know why their Friday outings to the pub had diminished. The double life started to eat at Theo. He loved Sasha with every fiber of his being, but how could he give himself to her fully when she didnıt know the most fundamental thing about his existence? She had no idea how difficult it was at times to suppress the magic in his veins. She had no idea the horrors heıd survived as a child. She had no idea the horrors heıd survived at his boarding school at the hands of his fatherıs master and cronies. She had no idea that had the Dark side won, he would have been expected to fall in line with his deranged father. He would have been expected to torture and kill people like Sasha and her family simply because they were non-magical. And so when their talks drifted into the territory of houses, or futures, or forever, Theoıs heart simultaneously soared in joy and clenched in panic. Sasha was everything heıd ever wanted: beautiful, smart, driven, friendly, kind, funny, and for some reason, as in love with Theo as he was with her. But now heıd arrived at a crossroads. To tell, or not to tell? Keep up the charade or close this chapter? Because the questions werenıt stopping and they werenıt getting any easier to deflect. He really didnıt have any friends from school? Just exactly where in Scotland was this Hogwarts located? Funny name for a school, donıt you think? Why hadnıt he attended a university? Why had she never been introduced to any of Theoıs work colleagues? Theo was at a breaking point and since heıd neglected to tell his healer that the love of his life, the woman he wanted to marry, was actually a Muggle for fear of the course of action the healer would advise, Theo had impulsively owled Draco. Once the alcohol started flowing, he couldnıt stop himself from revealing secret after secret, hoping that one of his oldest friends might understand what Theo sought with Sasha: a chance to finally live on his own terms. A chance that both he and Draco might have had if they hadnıt been born into their cursed pureblood families. With their fathers gone, they finally could plan the lives they wanted without needing a formal contract to marry or seek a career or breathe without permission. Theo met Dracoıs questioning gray eyes with desperate brown ones, needing the help of his friend, but not knowing how to ask. When Theo finished his tale, Draco stared back helplessly. His friend had fallen head over heels for a Muggle and Dracoıs brain scrambled to come to terms with this fact. ÊSo thatıs it, thatıs my sorry story,Ë Theo said glumly. ÊItıs why Iıve been a ghost and a shitty friend to you. Merlin, this is some situation Iıve landed myself in.Ë Draco pitied his friend but remained lost as to how to make this better. He also couldnıt help but feel as if all of this could have been avoided if theyıd just asked one simple question growing up: why? Muggles are inferior, Draco. Why? We must serve the Dark Lord. Why? You must marry a witch with the proper heritage upon graduation and produce an heir as soon as possible. Why? Why? Why? Why? ÊTheo, mate I╔ I truly donıt know what to say.Ë Theo let out a derisive snort. ÊI guess I should have figured that. The great sole heir to the spoils of the house of Malfoy is not exactly the person Iıd expect to approve of my predicament.Ë ÊExcuse me? Listen hereÐË ÊYouıre excused. Look, I didnıt unload all this onto you to hear a lecture.Ë ÊLecture?Ë ÊAbout how foolish Iım being to get myself involved with a Muggle woman.Ë ÊOf course itıs foolish but thatıs not what IÐË ÊBut what I really donıt need from you, Draco, is a tirade about how Iım sullying myself with Sasha. I truly hoped youıd evolved enough by now to not think that way anymore, but perhaps I was mistaken. One word about how filthy you think Muggles are and I wonıt hesitate to curse that nose off your poncey face.Ë Draco clenched his jaw and tried to get a word in while also trying to hide how his friendıs lack of confidence hurt. ÊTheo,Ë he ground out dangerously. ÊYou need to listen to me.Ë ÊNo I really donıt. Weıve shared enough over the years about our despicable families, enough for me to know how messed up all that pureblood ideology made you, but I can see now I was wrong and your fatherıs reach is too strong for you not to have turned out just like him.Ë ÊEnough!Ë Draco spat in such a startlingly accurate imitation of Lucius that he finally succeeded in shutting Theo up. ÊIf you donıt let me speak Iım leaving and Iım not coming back, understood Nott?Ë Theo nodded but frowned warily. ÊFor the record, your lack of faith in our friendship is fucking astounding and itıs going to take several more bottles of whisky for us to get past that,Ë Draco began and ran a shaky hand through his hair. ÊBut Iıll try to help you,Ë Draco murmured, trying and failing to keep the hurt out of his voice. ÊIıll try. I owe you, you know, after everything you did for me. All those times I Flooed over, blackout drunk and raving, trying to ignore that burning call in my blood for just one more potion? Youıve seen me at my absolute worst, did you not think Iıd ever repay that favor?Ë Theo had the decency to look abashed. ÊIım sorry, I know╔ Iım sorry. Itıs only that╔. I want to protect her, from everything, from my old life and I donıt know who I can trust. I donıt know how sheıll react if she ever finds out about my family, about our role in the war. Sheıd hate me╔ Iıd hate me.Ë His voice trailed off in a resigned whisper. Draco sighed and sipped his whisky. Theo had shown a willingness to be vulnerable, and Draco owed his friend some truth in return. ÊI understand better than you might think. Iım seeing someone,Ë Draco stated bluntly, staring down into his glass. ÊOh! Erm, good for you mate! Who?Ë Piquing Theoıs curiosity seemed to pull him out of his melancholy. ÊGranger. Hermione Granger.Ë Draco looked up to meet his friendıs eyes and was puzzled to find him grinning. ÊHermione Granger?Ë ÊYes.Ë Theoıs grin only widened, his thin lips stretched as far as they could go. Then without warning, he threw his head back in a violent peal of laughter. There were actual tears coming out of his eyes as he continued to guffaw, drawing more than a few stares from other bar patrons. Draco recast the Sound-Interfering Charm with an irritated flick of his wand. ÊOh╔ oh Merlin╔ oh that was a good one.Ë Theo chuckled and wiped his eyes. ÊI fail to see the humor in the fact that Iım seeing Granger,Ë Draco said stonily, putting on his iciest glare of intimidation but Theo would not lose his stupid grin of disbelief. ÊYeah, yeah, no one in their right mind would actually believe that.Ë Draco leveled him with an unblinking stare. ÊSome days I donıt even believe it,Ë he replied softly and Theoıs eyes widened as the smile slid off his face. ÊYouıre serious, arenıt you? But╔ but she hated you. And you well╔ you werenıt quite nice to her.Ë Draco snorted. ÊThatıs putting it lightly.Ë ÊAnd she╔ forgave you?Ë Draco rolled his eyes. ÊObviously,Ë he drawled. ÊHow long have you been seeing her?Ë ÊThat part is relatively new I suppose╔ a month, month and a half?Ë Did one start counting a relationship from the first shag? This was a question probably best left to Granger. ÊAnd how did you convince her to give you the time of day?Ë ÊWeıve been friends for a while now, even before I apologized for everything, and then things sort of╔ progressed naturally from there.Ë Theo regarded Draco with a mixture of surprise and pride. ÊMerlin Malfoy this is╔ this is fantastic! This is fucking brilliant!Ë ÊIım not following.Ë Theo set his glass down, eyes now alight with the beginnings of a plan. ÊThis is how you can help me! Think about it, Granger is quite possibly the most intelligent witch since Rowena Ravenclaw, right?Ë ÊSheıs been known to have a few good ideas,Ë Draco responded wryly. ÊAnd sheıs Muggleborn!Ë ÊYouıve lost me.Ë Theo began gesturing wildly with his hands, willing Draco to see the brilliance of his idea. ÊDonıt you see? I can introduce you two to Sasha so she thinks I have friends!Ë ÊI am your friend, you git.Ë Theo waved away this statement as a mere trifle of a fact. ÊWhatever. Look, we can go on a double date! Granger will be brilliant like she always is, and obviously being raised by Muggles sheıll know how to act in front of Sasha and Iım sure she can keep you in line for an evening.Ë ÊYour confidence in me is truly touching. Iıll have you know Iıve been out and about in the Muggle world and have yet to violate the Statute of Secrecy.Ë Draco neglected to mention the incident just last weekend when he vanished a spilled glass of wine in a restaurant and Hermione gave him a complete dressing down about how lucky he was that no one had seen. ÊSo youıll do it then? Youıll ask Granger for me?Ë Draco huffed out a breath. ÊFine, Iıll help you keep lying to your little Muggle girlfriend. Donıt expect Granger to be happy about it.Ë Theo looked so genuinely glad at Dracoıs agreement that he couldnıt help but also feel something akin to joy. He had his friend back and had finally been able to tell another soul about his relationship that wasnıt paid to listen to him. Theo raised a freshly poured glass of whisky in his direction. ÊTo Granger. Not sure what she sees in you, but honestly, well done.Ë Draco rolled his eyes and accepted his friendıs toast. Things got progressively blurry after that. With the melancholic brooding portion of the evening completed, the two old friends began toasting everything (Êhonest to Salazar, McGonagall was the best professor at that blasted school, to McGonagall!Ë) and anything (Êtelevision is ruddy brilliant, to telly!Ë) as they shared a second and then a third bottle of Ogdenıs. Theo interrogated Draco about the budding romance between him and his former nemesis (Theoıs words, Draco would never deign to be so dramatic), Dracoıs tongue getting looser with the increased consumption of alcohol. ÊI should send her flowers!Ë Theo exclaimed suddenly. ÊWho?Ë ÊGranger, obviously!Ë ÊWhatÐwhyÐwhyıre youÐsending my GrangerÐmy girlfriendÐflowers?Ë Draco slurred and finished with a hiccough. ÊBecause! Sheıs so helpful! Yıknow with all the Muggle stuff. I have to show her my gratÐ gratitude. Sheıs smart, your girl.Ë The normally quiet and reserved Theo was what one would call a hyperactive and overly excitable drunk. Draco on the other hand, generally landed more on the morose drunk side of the spectrum, but his friendıs enthusiasm was contagious tonight. ÊYes! Yes. Women loveÐflowersÐyes. Flowers! Oh Merlin,Ë Draco set his drink down and looked panicked. ÊOh Merlinıs arse, do you know Iıve never given her flowers?Ë ÊNot once? Whatıs herÐfavÐfavorite fl-flower?Ë Draco put his head in his hands in defeat. ÊIıve no bloody idea! Fuck, what is wrong with me!? Yıknow she smells like hyaÐhyacinths╔ Granger does.Ë Oh gods, he was the worst, just the absolute worst romantic partner in the history of relationships, he thought blearily. ÊAh mate, youıre failing the most basic of pureblood etiquette rules. Youıve not sent the woman youıre courting flowers! Mine loves daisies. Loves Èem.Ë ÊI think weıre failing a lot of pureblood rules, donıt you?Ë chortled Draco, suddenly chipper again. ÊFeels good, yeah?Ë Theo joined in with a snigger. ÊFeels fucking fantastic. Look at us! Well, somewhat. Apprehensively anticipated was probably more accurate. True, he looked forward to finally introducing someone in his life to Hermione. He had an inkling that Theo and Hermione would hit it off. Cozying up with a Death Eater?Ë Padma gasped as Harry and Ginny made noises in protest while Hermione shot back, ÊDonıt call him that!Ë ÊWell why not? Thatıs what he is, Hermione! Heıs the same as his father!Ë ÊHeıs not!Ë Hermione shouted, more tears tracking their way down her cheeks. ÊHeıs not,Ë she repeated in a quiet voice. ÊI think a certain mark on his forearm might beg to differ,Ë Ron responded coldly. ÊHe didnıt have a choice Ron! Voldemort threatened to kill his parents!Ë Hermione replied desperately, trying to appeal to Ronıs forgiving nature. But Ron merely snorted derisively. ÊDonıt try to convince me he didnıt want that life, he only regrets ending up on the losing side. You honestly believe he feels bad about that decision?Ë ÊI know he does,Ë Hermione said firmly. ÊYouıre blind, Hermione, absolutely blind,Ë Ron said sadly. ÊIf Harry and I hadnıt broken out of their Manor cellar that night I bet you anything he would have traded off with his dear Auntie Bella when she got tired of torturing you herself.Ë Hermione inhaled sharply and felt as if sheıd been slapped. Distantly she heard Ginny snarl while Harry shouted, ÊRon, thatıs enough! Donıt speak to Hermione like that, what is wrong with you?Ë Ron paid him no mind, and focused his gaze on Hermione. ÊNo, this is between me and her. She knows╔ she knows what that night╔ how that night affected me.Ë Seeing his haunted blue eyes, hearing his voice break, she felt it then, the burn of shame. Ron thought it his greatest failing, being unable to shield Hermione from Bellatrix. Theyıd suffered through many a night together where Ron woke up screaming Hermioneıs name in pure terror, dreaming of being dragged away from her into the cellar of Malfoy Manor on the worst night of his life and hers. Hermione couldnıt look away from Ron, even as she felt her heart shattering. Ron, who had held her during the night terrors, who stemmed her tears and fetched Calming Draughts, who cleaned her vomit and dabbed at her sweaty forehead when she succumbed to the worst of her memories╔ he now looked at her in true agony. How could you keep this from me, Hermione? After everything we were to each other? How could you? His family almost tore you apart, what are you thinking? ÊHe had a wand,Ë Ron said shakily. ÊHe had his wand Hermione, and he did nothing. You could have bled out right at his feet and he wouldnıt have lifted a finger.Ë Hermione still refused to back down. ÊHe would have been killed, Ron.Ë ÊSO WHAT?Ë Ron roared. ÊI would have gladly been killed for you! Remember that, Hermione. I would have died rather than see you tortured like that!Ë A ringing silence punctuated his shouted words and Padma laid a placating hand on Ronıs arm. He shook her off and ran a hand through his hair. ÊDo youÐË he swallowed thickly but plowed on. ÊDo you love him?Ë ÊYes.Ë Four pairs of eyes snapped to her face at the quickness of her answer. Ron shook his head, disappointed and disgusted. ÊNever took you for a fool, Hermione.Ë Ginny began to loudly protest, but Ron ignored her again. With a deadened look in his eyes, he placed Padmaıs container and the bottle of champagne on the kitchen table. ÊHere,Ë he said flatly. ÊWe were going to announce the good news tonight. Padma and I are moving in together... but I canıt be here right now.Ë Ron turned on his heel and stalked out of the kitchen. Padma whispered an embarrassed apology and hurried after him. Harry looked to Ginny for help. ÊGo,Ë his wife urged him at once. ÊGo after him, make sure heıs all right.Ë As Harry rushed to the Floo, Ginny directed Hermioneıs head onto her shoulder. The affectionate, sisterly gesture brought on a fresh wave of tears for Hermione, and the red head let her cry silently, making soothing noises. Did Ron have a point? Could she really have been so naòve? She closed her eyes and pressed the heels of her palms to her eyelids, trying to calm her swirling mind and racing heart. She knew why Ron felt so hurt and betrayed by her and Harry right now. Ron only recalled Malfoy as the petulant bully who did everything in his power to make Ron feel worthless. He had no concept of the more mature man Malfoy had worked so hard to become, had no idea the demons he wrestled with, how horrified he would be now to be compared to Lucius. And could she really blame Ron for his overreaction? Harry and Ginny had each had time and first-hand experiences to reconcile the two versions of Draco, but this had been sprung out of nowhere on an unsuspecting Ron. No, to Ron, Draco was the smirking prat who had no problem sneering at the less fortunate and firing off morally questionable hexes at schoolmatesı backs. Over a year and a half ago, would Hermione not have thought the same? Back then, the name ÊDraco MalfoyË would have conjured memories of a snarling pale face hurling the word ÊMudbloodË in her direction. Now, however, the name ÊDraco MalfoyË conjured memories of his lips trailing soft kisses down her body, of his face between her thighs, of spirited debates over morning coffee, of good-natured teasing about Muggle music, of the way he passionately breathed her given name, of his gray eyes burning with need and unspoken declarations, of his fingers playing with hers, of him holding her when he let his guard down, of him admitting weaknesses and faults, of him asking her to forgive him╔ Because she had forgiven him. All the way back in November heıd asked her forgiveness and sheıd given it freely. ÊAre you all right?Ë asked Ginny. ÊNo.Ë ÊRon had no right to say those things to you.Ë Hermione sniffled and gave a half-shrug of her shoulders. ÊHeıs hurting, I understand why heıs so upset. Ron was always sensitive about me and Harry keeping anything from him after╔ after╔Ë ÊAfter he left you both on the run?Ë Ginny finished for her. Hermione nodded. ÊHeıs never really forgiven himself for that. Add in his complex about Malfoy╔ well this was the perfect storm, really, to set Ron off.Ë Harry reappeared suddenly in the kitchen. ÊPadma told me Iıd just missed him. Sheıs pretty sure he went round to Georgeıs. She reckons that might be the best place for him right now.Ë Hermione worried her bottom lip between her teeth, feeling guilty that sheıd ruined a perfectly good dinner as well as Ron and Padmaıs excitement over the next step in their relationship. Harry noticed Hermioneıs anxious habit and frowned. ÊGin, can I speak to Hermione alone for a second?Ë Ginny gave Hermioneıs shoulders a soft squeeze, pecked Harry on the cheek and disappeared into the living room, closing the kitchen door behind her. Harry sighed and took Ginnyıs vacated seat next to Hermione. He rubbed his temple then folded his hands on the table in front of him. ÊI need you to know that I donıt agree with Ron. Gin and I both support you completely, no matter who you choose to date, and even if╔ even if Malfoy and I donıt exactly╔ like one another╔ I would never stand in the way of you being with someone you╔ love.Ë Harry looked as if the last word pained him to say, but he got it out nonetheless. ÊOh, Harry,Ë Hermione said weakly in gratitude, unable to say anything else. Harry shook his head, dismissing her unspoken apology and thanks. ÊEnough about Ronıs feelings. How can I help you with╔ whatever happened with Malfoy? You said he didnıt say anything, but you seemed awfully upset.Ë ÊHe didnıt, he tried to convince me to stay but I was too shocked and upset andÐË Hermione closed her eyes as the horrific realization dawned on her. ÊOh God, Harry, I just turned tail and ran! I didnıt even give him the benefit of the doubt, I assumed the worst of him when I saw his mother standing there.Ë The shock of Draco and his mother together in the room with her had plunged her backwards in time to the horrific memory of her torture at the Manor. Sheıd flown into a terrified panic before she could truly process that Draco didnıt feel that way anymore. She groaned and opened her eyes to meet Harryıs patient stare. ÊI really buggered this up, didnıt I?Ë Harry adjusted his glasses and cleared his throat. ÊWell, Iım not sure about that, but I think you should at least explain to Malfoy why you left the way you did. I know he doesnıt╔ I mean I donıt think he has a lot of people he can depend on in his life, besides you. And with you being a fairly recent source of support╔ Iım sure heıs probably feeling rather lonely right about now.Ë Hermione sighed and wiped her face dry. Timidly, she voiced the fear that had been plaguing her since the run-in with Narcissa. ÊDo you think we have a chance? Draco and me?Ë Harry shrugged, but then reached over and held one of Hermioneıs hands between his own. ÊThatıs not really up to me, is it? Iıll support you no matter what you decide.Ë Hermione let out a watery chuckle. ÊHarry Potter you are getting sappy in your old age.Ë He responded with a boyish grin. ÊWell I am going to be a father, so I guess I need to practice this whole talking-about-feelings thing.Ë Feeling as if her legs would be able to support her weight again, Hermione stood and pulled her hand from Harryıs. ÊLook,Ë Harry said, a bit awkward. ÊI cannot believe Iım about to say this but, you might want to hear Malfoy out. Despite everything that happened╔ I think he really is trying to change, or uhh, has changed.Ë Hermione regarded him in shock. What happened to the suspicious boy who was so convinced of Dracoıs wrongdoings he ordered two house elves to tail him during their Sixth Year? As if Harry could hear her thoughts, he pressed on. ÊHermione if I learned anything at all from the mess that was my youth, it was that people can surprise you. Even those you thought might not be capable of love or of change╔ itıs best not to jump to conclusions, yeah?Ë ÊI understand. I think I should be getting on now.Ë Harry walked her through to the living room. ÊSee you at The Burrow tomorrow?Ë ÊOf course. Tell Gin thanks and Iım sorry I ruined the evening.Ë ÊYou didnıt. You okay going home? Youıre welcome to stay here you know.Ë Hermione hugged him tightly. ÊI know, Harry. But I think itıs Crookshanksı turn to deal with my emotions. Iıll see you tomorrow.Ë Chapter End Notes A/N: Some italicized dialogue taken directly from The Half-Blood Prince. Thank you everyone for reading! I only just now figured out how to turn on anonymous asks. Chapter 31: Chapter31 Chapter Notes TRIGGER WARNING FOR THIS CHAPTER: Detailed description of a panic attack and its side effects. See the end of the chapter for more notes She should still have some strawberry ice cream in her freezer and more than a few bottles of wine to choose from at home. But when Hermione stepped out of her Floo, all thoughts of drowning her sorrows in dessert and alcohol were summarily dashed. Had she not been experiencing a complete emotional breakdown the entire evening, the sight that greeted her in her kitchen would have made her laugh until her sides split. Draco and Crookshanks each occupied a chair at her kitchen table, a stack of parchment in between, looking to all the world like two men having a very serious and sophisticated discussion about the state of global economics. ÊBut what your argument fails to consider, my good man, is the impact this legislation would have on the export relationship with China.Ë ÊNo, no, see, you must understand the boost this would give the home market. Itıs quite simple, really.Ë Both pairs of eyes flicked to Hermione as she appeared in the entryway, pulling her from her ludicrous reverie. Draco got uncertainly to his feet. ÊHello,Ë he said softly. ÊHi,Ë she replied weakly, immediately self-conscious of her bedraggled appearance. Her hair was likely a ratıs nest, her clothes still rumpled from the Floo, and she didnıt even want to know the state of her barely dry eyes. Yet there Draco stood, with his perfectly crisp navy suit and not a lock of his platinum hair out of place, frustrating Hermione on several levels. Not something sheıd normally complain about, but the manıs enduring attractiveness was a most unfair advantage when she looked like a half-drowned kneazle. ÊHow was dinner with your mother?Ë It came out more bitterly than she intended, but Draco merely shrugged at her question. ÊI wouldnıt know, I left shortly after you did.Ë Hermioneıs jaw dropped. ÊHave you been here all this time?Ë Draco shrugged again. ÊI think I missed you by about two minutes.Ë ÊBut then that means youıve╔ youıve been here for hours!Ë She exclaimed, catching a glimpse of the wall clock. Draco didnıt respond, just fixed her with that penetrating gray stare. The naked emotion in his eyes overwhelmed her, and she turned away. ÊWould you like tea? I could use some myself.Ë She bustled by him to fiddle with the kettle on the stove, doing everything she could to distract herself and avoid looking at him. ÊNo, thank you. Perhaps we could sit and talk?Ë His too polite, too calm demeanor disrupted both Hermioneıs preconceived behavioral expectations as well as her conviction to lose herself in the mundane social ritual of preparing a pot of tea. Why wasnıt he upset with her? Why wasnıt he shouting and demanding to know where sheıd been? She looked a right mess and yet he had the audacity to appear perfectly put together? Hermione flicked her wand to heat the kettle. ÊFine,Ë she said tersely, and walked by him to the couch and sat down primly. She knew it wasnıt fair, but the lack of emotional response from Draco started to irritate her. He should be the one itching to fly off the handle, not her. An annoyingly and unusually placid Draco settled into the armchair facing her. No escaping his heated looks now, to turn her head away would be admitting defeat. ÊDid you really follow right after me?Ë she asked, disbelievingly. ÊI did.Ë ÊAnd what did your mother have to say about my presence in your home?Ë ÊNothing of importance.Ë Draco leant forward to rest his elbows on his knees. ÊMy priority was you tonight.Ë Hermione felt tears prick at her eyes and redirected her fury inwards, finding herself so unprepared to deal with his quiet sincerity that she completely lost control of her tear ducts again. Heıd put her first tonight and what did she do? Sheıd run away like a child, hiding from her problems. The first sign of trouble and sheıd bolted, while Draco stayed behind, waiting for her to recover from her fit of immaturity. How had this happened? When did she let herself become so weak? ÊI take it you went to the Potters?Ë he asked softly. ÊYes. I needed╔ I just needed to speak to Harry and Ginny but then╔ but then Ron showed up.Ë Dracoıs eyes immediately hardened. ÊI see,Ë he clipped. ÊDid you have a lovely evening with all your old friends?Ë Finally, heıd allowed some semblance of emotion to seep through. She didnıt miss the bitter resentment in his question at the thought of Hermione running to Ron with a problem. Especially a problem involving relationship matters. ÊIt was awful, actually,Ë Hermione snapped. ÊAnd Iım sure youıll be thrilled to know that Ron completely lost it when I told him we were together!Ë Why was she so hell bent on trying to rile him up? The comment about Ron hit its mark and she saw a muscle twitch in Dracoıs clenched jaw. ÊOn the contrary,Ë he hissed, ÊI find myself rather more concerned about the state you arrived in just now. Did he hurt you? What did he say to you?Ë Part of Hermione warmed at the possessive nature of his questioning but sheıd had about enough of the men in her life reigniting old feuds tonight. She stood suddenly and crossed her arms over her chest. ÊHe was upset that I kept our relationship from him, and yes, he lashed out with some unkind words, but heıs only trying to protect me!Ë Hermione hadnıt meant to excuse Ronıs cruel actions, surprising even herself at the way she quickly tried to justify his intentions. She had no idea why she defended Ronıs poor behavior to Draco, but all her insidious doubts and fears about falling for the man in front of her couldnıt seem to disappear. Was I wrong about you? Was I looking for someone who wasnıt really there? ÊOh yes, Iım sure the Weasel delighted in reminding you about all my past transgressions. Let me guess, heıs adamant that Iım incapable of change? Still believes I exist to prey on those that arenıt pureblood?Ë ÊIs that so wrong of him? Have you ever given him a reason not to think that?Ë She immediately wished the callous retort never left her mouth. Draco looked stung for a moment but quickly tried to mask the hurt. He closed his eyes, taking a deep breath and running his hands through his hair. In the awful silence that followed her unkindness, Hermione debated closing the distance to comfort him, but couldnıt get her limbs to move, paralyzed with shame and awaiting the inevitable fallout. Draco moved first, slowly rising to his feet, looking down at her with a pained expression. After a few more beats of silence he drew his wand. ÊAccio parchment,Ë he said quietly and Hermione didnıt miss the way his hands lightly shook. The neat stack of papers that had been curiously sitting on her kitchen table zoomed into his shaky grip. Dracoıs pale face looked nervous but determined as he held it out for her to take. ÊWhatıs this?Ë ÊA reason.Ë Hermione unfolded the bundle and read the top line with a furrowed brow. She read the words written in bold, black ink at the top of the first page once, twice, then a third time, her normally quick mind unable to comprehend what sheıd read. Her eyes scanned the rest of the first page of what appeared to be a funding agreement for the genesis of something extraordinary titled The Hermione J. Granger Fund for Students of Non-Magical Parents. Hermione looked back up at Draco, confused. ÊI donıt understand╔ what is all this? Why does this fund have my name associated with it? Iıve never even heard of it!Ë When he didnıt answer, she flipped through more of the pages, stunned to discover it mirrored her greatest wishes for how a program like this could work for future students of Hogwarts. In fact, most of the language and ideas were ripped word for word from her private notebook. Beyond that, she noticed, reading further and further on, some additional tenets had been added (providing actual magical creatures to introduce to the children? Brilliant!) to expand the scope of her initial dream even further. She held a contract, she finally realized, reaching the bottom of the last page with blank lines for two signatures. ÊWhat does this all mean?Ë she breathed, not daring to voice her fantastical suspicion. Draco reached inside his suit jacket and produced a letter. ÊThis might clear up any confusion,Ë he intoned, still anxious. Hermione unfurled the letter and read: Dear Mr. Malfoy, It is with sincere pleasure that I enclose the Ministry-approved contract for The Hermione J. Granger Fund for Students of Non-Magical Parents. You may keep this copy for your records. Once you and Miss Granger have signed, the copy in my possession will reflect your signatures, and the funds released from your vaults. On behalf of the professors, the Board of Governors, and the future pupils of Hogwarts, thank you for your generosity. On a personal note, I enjoyed collaborating with you on this new endeavor, and I do hope Miss Granger appreciates all of your hard work on her cause. I shall be in touch about preparations for the inaugural gala. Best, Minerva McGonagall Headmistress, Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry P.S. When you see Miss Granger, please give her my best and I do hope sheıll forgive me for my part in keeping this temporarily confidential. By the time sheıd reached the end of the letter, Hermione had succumbed to crying again. ÊWhy╔ why have I got to sign?Ë she asked weakly. Draco tapped one of the signature lines with a long, pale finger. ÊBecause youıre Executive Director and nothing happens without your approval.Ë ÊAnd,Ë she swallowed the lump in her throat, Êand the other line is for you?Ë He delicately took the parchment from between her hands. ÊMay I?Ë She nodded mutely and watched in amazement as he brandished the gold fountain pen sheıd gifted him at Christmas and smoothly signed his full name on the line. The black ink letters reading Draco Lucius Malfoy flashed a bright gold color the second heıd finished, then reappeared in black again, this time reading as Anonymous Benefactor. ÊYou donıt have to sign tonight if youıd like to look it over more thoroughly,Ë he told her quietly. ÊI made a deal with McGonagall to not show you until we knew the School Governors and the Ministry were on board. If you have questions, Iım sure McGonagall would be more than happy toÐË Hermione abruptly snatched the pen and the parchment out of his hand. Slamming the paper on the coffee table, she hastily scrawled her signature and then threw the pen down triumphantly. Draco gaped at her, astonished. ÊBut╔ donıt you want to go over every line? Make sure you agree with every facet?Ë ÊNo need,Ë she breathed. ÊI trust you.Ë She stepped up to him and kissed him fiercely. As her hands wound their way around his taut shoulders, she felt the tension dissipating as his body sagged with relief and he relaxed into her kiss. She pulled away reluctantly, but the lure of all her questions became too strong to ignore. ÊThatıs what tonight was all about, wasnıt it? YouÐyou were planning to tell me at dinner.Ë ÊYes,Ë he confirmed. ÊBut then Mother unfortunately showed up right before you were due to arrive and I didnıt have time to explain everything to her before I could intercept you. Timing was really not on my side tonight.Ë Everything hit Hermione at once and she felt the despairing shame of her actions again. Sheıd ruined the beautiful evening heıd planned and at the first hint of conflict, sheıd run. Whatıs more, instead of throwing the blame at her feet, heıd chased her down and waited here all night for her to gather her senses. Harryıs honest words echoed in her mind: I donıt think he has a lot of people in his life that he can depend on. While Hermione ran off questioning the validity of the last few months, Draco had remained steadfast in his faith in their relationship; willing to fight for her, for them. And how had she repaid him? Stomped all over his trust, knowing his vulnerabilities, knowing he didnıt let his guard down to just anyone, and sheıd gone and believed the worst about him when heıd given her no cause to do so. Hermione felt her eyes water again and cursed her propensity for tears tonight. Merlin, hadnıt all the moisture leaked out of her body by now? ÊHow╔ how long have you been working on this fund?Ë A pink tinge appeared on Dracoıs pallid cheekbones. ÊErr╔ I first visited McGonagall to pitch the idea╔ your idea, I mean╔ back in October.Ë ÊOctober,Ë she repeated faintly. October. Before they were dating, before theyıd even defined themselves as friends, before heıd worked up the nerve to ask for her forgiveness, Draco had already begun quietly working to upend societyıs expectations of him. Heıd been changing right before her eyes and sheıd barely given him credit. All those mornings in the cafÄ when sheıd ranted about the appalling lack of education for Muggle families with magical children, he hadnıt just been half-listening or paying her lip service with his comments. Heıd been moved enough by her passion to engineer an entire charity with her vision in mind. ÊDraco,Ë she sniffled. ÊThis is the most thoughtful thing anyone has ever done for me. IÐIım so sorry I left, please, forgive me. Please, Iım so sorry.Ë Draco didnıt answer, looking pained at the sight of more tears on her face. Taking a step back, Hermione wiped at her puffy, streaming eyes. ÊWhy? Why did you do all of this?Ë ÊBecause I╔ I╔Ë He seemed conflicted about how to answer, his eyes darting around the room. ÊBecause I knew how much you wanted something like this to exist, but you hardly had the time or the resources to bring it to fruition.Ë A good response and very sweet, especially for Draco, but Hermione detected the slight detour from complete honesty. She knew what heıd refrained from saying, what heıd almost said. I love you, too. Letting him off the hook for now, Hermione wrapped her arms around him and rested her head against his chest. ÊIım so sorry for ruining tonight,Ë she murmured. Strong, nimble fingers ghosted down her jaw and tilted her face up. The relief that shone in his eyes only fed her guilt that she could be so cruel as to doubt him. ÊYou were having a panic attack, itıs perfectly understandable.Ë Hermione shook her head firmly. ÊNo, Draco, no. I owe you an apologyÐË ÊYou donıt, please, donıt apologize, you donıt owe meÐË ÊI do!Ë Hermione cut him off sternly. ÊI apologize for leaving. It was unfair to you. Should I become upset or angry with you in the future, I promise not to leave. Iım sorry.Ë Discomfort ran rampant across his features, despite his best efforts to maintain an unaffected air. ÊItıs fine Granger, had I known my mother was a trigger for you, I would haveÐË ÊNo, Draco, if this relationship is going to work, you need to learn to accept apologies from me when I have wronged you. And to be clear,Ë she pressed up on her toes and kissed him softly, ÊI want this to work.Ë His eyes roved over her face, drinking her in with almost an intent to memorize. Draco regarded her smiling face with a contradictory combination of both hunger and satiation; the look of a man who truly thought he might have lost her, and even now as she stood before him after declaring her commitment, he still could not fully come to terms with a deserved offering of stable, consistent happiness. ÊI accept your apology,Ë he gruffly replied. He obviously required more reassurance, but Hermione sensed he would neither voice the insecurity, nor articulate his own needs at this time. ÊI donıt tell you enough, do I?Ë she asked, mirroring his question from the other night. ÊTell me what?Ë ÊThat youıre a good man, Draco.Ë A scream tore through the otherwise silent bedroom. Hermione bolted upright, her wand already in hand, ready to either defend or attack. Blinking away the last vestiges of sleep, she looked quickly around the room before glancing beside her to check on Draco. The terrifying shout had originated from him, and he let out another anguished yell, his long limbs jerking and twisting in the sheets. His face was paler than normal and contorted in pain, while his hands clenched into fists so tight Hermione worried he might bleed from digging his own nails into his palms. ÊNO! NO! PLEASE!Ë Hermione leapt into action, tossing her wand aside and placing her hands firmly on the sides of his sweat-soaked face. ÊDraco! Wake up! Itıs just a dream, wake up!Ë His body trembled fitfully for a moment more before his eyes snapped open. His gaze fixed on her, but panicked and scared. ÊGra-Granger?Ë He slurred, as if he couldnıt understand that she really was in front of him and not a lasting image from his nightmare. ÊIım here, itıs all right, youıre safe, it was only a dream,Ë she murmured soothingly and ran her fingers through his hair. But Dracoıs eyes still darted around the room, his chest heaving as he breathed in too hard and too fast. Body still shaking, he sat up so rapidly he almost collided with Hermioneıs head. She pulled away just in time as he swung his long legs over the side of the bed and promptly vomited on the floor. Hermione immediately vanished the sick and conjured a bucket. Sheıd unfortunately logged so many nights of experience with this exact situation (both as victim and caretaker) during her relationship with Ron that these steps were second-nature. Dracoıs shoulders shook as he retched into the bucket again and Hermione summoned a wash cloth and dampened it with water from her wand. The moonlight filtering in through the window reflected off the glistening skin of his back, and Hermione fought down her own heartache at the sight of Draco succumbing to his nightmares. She touched the cloth to the back of his neck, and though he tried to shrug her off at first, another bout of sickness required his attention and he surrendered to her gesture of comfort. She rubbed his back soothingly and pushed his hair off his sweat-covered brow, while his body hunched and continued to empty his stomachıs contents into the receptacle. His knuckles turned white against the rim, and Hermione wondered if it would crack under the pressure of his grip. She alternated between vanishing his sick and gently pressing the wash cloth to his neck and face. Eventually, his body spent, Hermione tugged the bucket from his grip, vanishing it all together. She shoved a glass of water into his hands and continued to murmur words of comfort in his ear. Too tired to be embarrassed now, he abandoned his earlier feeble attempts to fend her off, and sank into her arms. Hermione scooted up to the edge of the bed beside him as Dracoıs weight sagged against her side. He only managed a few gulps of the water before he dropped the glass, his frame still suffering mild convulsions and aftershocks. Hermione held him all the tighter, repeating over and over, ÊIım here Draco, youıre safe.Ë When she felt something hot and wet drip onto her shoulder, she moved to grab the cloth to dab away more sweat, but a quick glimpse of his face revealed tears instead. From what she could see of Dracoıs face, his eyes were squeezed shut and his jaw clenched tight, but he could no longer hold the tears at bay. ÊTell me, please. Please Draco, what was it?Ë He took a shuddering breath that threatened to turn hysterical. Hermione placed a hand on his chest and counted a few breaths for him. ÊIt was Bellatrix,Ë he finally rasped. ÊShe had you╔ she had you under her wand and you were╔ you were╔Ë He broke off with a gasp and wrenched himself away from her to bury his head in his hands. He rocked his body back and forth as he cried out, ÊI d-didnıt s-save you╔ I didnıt save you╔ I never save you!Ë Draco broke down completely and Hermione gathered him against her before he could move away. She met no resistance from him now, as he clung to her instead, his trembling hands scrabbling for purchase, seeking to find a tether anywhere he could reach. She guided his head to her shoulder while his body wracked with sobs and Hermione tried not to come undone herself. All she could do for Draco now was allow him to draw comfort and safety from her touch. His arms gripped her tightly as he lost all control of his emotions, weeping as he choked out apologies against her skin. ÊIım sorry Iım sorry Iım sorry Iım so fucking sorry╔Ë Not for the first time, Hermione cursed their generationıs lot in life. A sobering thought occurred to Hermione then: How many of her fellow witches and wizards suffered this way? How many of them had the help they needed? Hermione could only help the wizard in her arms, and hope that she could be enough to combat the darkness that threatened to overwhelm them both. She didnıt interrogate Draco any further about his nightmare, a familiar one to her after all, but let him cry until his eyes ran dry. She kept her fingers running delicately through his hair all the while, never ceasing the rhythm as he practically howled in misery. Some time later, Draco relaxed under her ministrations and regulated his breathing. Sniffling a bit, he pulled away and dried his face with the heels of his hands. ÊI canıt do this Granger,Ë he said hoarsely. Hermione felt a sudden icy sting of dread, fearing the worst. What did that mean? Was he talking about them? About their relationship? Draco shook his head as he held it in his hands. ÊI canıt do this╔ I canıt be here. I need toÐI need╔Ë He looked up at Hermione then, eyes blazing. ÊI need to get out of here for a little while. Can we do that?Ë She met his intense gaze with a puzzled look. ÊWhat do you mean? Go where?Ë He clasped both her hands in his suddenly. ÊPlease, Granger,Ë he begged. ÊLetıs go away for a bit, a week tops. Just the two of us╔ out of England so I can fucking breathe again.Ë ÊYou want to go on holiday together?Ë ÊYes.Ë ÊWhen?Ë ÊRight now.Ë Hermione pulled her hands from his. ÊWhat? We canıt just leave! What about our jobs? Or Ð?Ë ÊFine, next weekend then!Ë She sat back and regarded him for a beat. ÊYouıre serious? How do you expect to just pick up and go to Merlin-knows-where andÐ?Ë ÊIıll take you to Paris. Fuck, Granger, Iıll take you to the bloody moon, just╔ please. If I donıt get off this tiny, damned island where everyone from my mother to fucking Weasley thinks they have the right to comment on my personal affairs then I am going to go fucking spare.Ë He looked at her so desperately then, his eyes still glassy and red-rimmed, that it left Hermione powerless to refuse him. She couldnıt remember the last time sheıd requested time off from work and truthfully, a holiday sounded rather lovely. No angry Ron to deal with, no judgmental pureblood mothers, just her and Draco. ÊAll right, we can take a trip,Ë she acquiesced. ÊWeıll talk more in the morning. Letıs get you back to bed.Ë Dracoıs expression cleared at her agreement and he allowed her to maneuver him back down into bed. His whole body sagged with both relief and exhaustion as Hermione propped an extra pillow under his head. ÊDo you want a Calming Draught?Ë He shook his head at her offer. ÊNo, I donıt think my stomach could handle any potions right now,Ë he responded weakly and Hermione could see sleep was already succeeding in claiming him. She gently dabbed at his forehead a few more times with the cool cloth as his eyes fluttered shut. She pushed away the dark thoughts about how Draco would have coped with these night terrors in the past, thankful that he could be vulnerable in front of her now. She laid a hand on his scalp and indulged in combing through his hair with her fingers, surprised but gratified when he leaned into her touch. ÊMmm, thank you love,Ë he murmured sleepily and Hermione stilled. Never, not once, had Draco ever addressed her by a term of endearment. Smiling to herself, Hermione settled down beside him. I love you, too. Chapter End Notes A/N: Thank you so much to everyone who continues on with this story, I love hearing from you all. Say hi any time on tumblr: @heyjude19-writing. Chapter 32: Chapter32 Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes Hermione took a deep breath and inhaled the usually comforting scent of a hot summer day outside of The Burrow. Now, however, her insides twisted in knots at the aroma, and not just because sheıd apparated on an empty stomach. Sheıd left Draco that morning with some ginger ale and toast, promising to be back later in the day so they could discuss this trip he was so desperate to take. Heıd grumpily waved her off and said heıd already decided on France and that heıd take care of everything, all she needed to do was tell her office she was taking all next week off. Hermione huffed and left, but had to admit, it would be quite nice to have someone else planning a trip instead of her. Any holiday sheıd taken with Ron had resulted in him complaining about her meticulous itineraries that sheıd spend weeks putting together. Speaking of Ron╔ Hermione wondered if heıd even be here today. Well, no use delaying the inevitable, she thought, and strode toward the home. ÊWell, you obviously have mine and Harryıs whole-hearted support. Has she even met Draco?Ë Hermione asked, bewildered as to how the French witch could possibly be upset by this. Ginny shot her an apologetic look. ÊFleur umm╔ well she blames Draco for Billıs injuries. Because he let Greyback into Hogwarts the night that Dumbledore died.Ë ÊOh.Ë She was torn between both amusement and indignation that the entire Weasley clan had seen fit to discuss her romantic relationship behind her back, going so far as to hold an emergency conclave. Thereıs not a person in this house that wouldnıt do anything for you.Ë ÊExcept let me make my own decisions about whom I date,Ë she muttered bitterly. Hermione pushed her hair back and shook off Ginnyıs hand. ÊIs everyone waiting inside, ready to either berate me or dole out unwanted advice?Ë ÊNo, everyone is in the backyard, waiting to see which hex you use on Ron first. For everything.Ë She straightened her shoulders and took another deep breath, then a thought struck. ÊHang on╔ your parents╔ what did they have to say?Ë ÊAh, see thatıs really why I came to intercept you. ÊHello Hermione dear,Ë she immediately engulfed her in a hug and Hermione relished in the maternal embrace. After the day and night sheıd endured, the comfort of a motherıs arms almost made her cry all over again. Molly pulled back and held Hermioneıs face tenderly in her hands. ÊIım not here to judge you or chastise you. I just want to talk. Tea?Ë Hermione nodded weakly and sank heavily into a chair. Out the kitchen window she could see all the assembled Weasleys in the backyard, but didnıt focus her gaze on any one in particular. Her attention was pulled back to the matriarch as she placed a steaming cup in front of her. ÊThe Malfoys and the Weasleys have always had bad blood between them, going back a few generations now. Even my own family, the Prewetts, didnıt care for them. When I was a girl, my father used to rage about Abraxas Malfoy and his interference at the Ministry.Ë She paused and sipped her tea delicately. ÊAnd as for Arthur and Lucius╔ well youıve seen firsthand the animosity between the pair of them.Ë Hermione snorted as she recalled the brawl between the two grown men at Flourish & Blotts before the start of her Second Year. ÊI know what Lucius used to say about Arthur. I know how he used to degrade my husband, besmirch his name to make sure he never advanced in his career. Lucius Malfoy was a spiteful, pitiful man, and I would be lying if I said I didnıt take some comfort in knowing heıs gone from this world.Ë Hermione nodded in understanding, silently telling the older woman she didnıt begrudge her this opinion. ÊAs for Narcissa╔ I canıt claim to know the woman very well. On the rare occasion our paths did cross I found she more than lived up to her reputation of being haughty and cold. And how a mother could simply stand by and allow her only child to be forced to serve that╔ that monster╔ well Iıll never understand that. I have no sympathy for the woman, however╔Ë Molly took a shaky inhale as her eyes filled with tears. Hermione placed a hand over hers and received a watery smile in return. ÊShe saved Harryıs life. And for that, should our paths ever cross again, I will gladly clasp her hands and thank her.Ë Hermione stared back in surprise. Molly sniffed and continued. ÊI would, dear, no matter her flaws, she did save that boyıs life, and for that, I owe her a debt of gratitude. Then thereıs the fact that I╔ that I╔ I murdered her sister,Ë she finished in almost a whisper and Hermione was dismayed to see a tear leak out. ÊMolly, no! You were defending your family, you were defending your child! Bellatrix was aiming to kill Ginny, to kill me! Do you have any idea how many lives you saved that day?Ë Molly shook her head sadly. ÊI still took a life and that is a decision I have to live with. Iıd do it again, but that doesnıt mean the choice doesnıt haunt me every now and then.Ë Hermione could only marvel at the strength and compassion of the older woman before her. Molly took out a worn handkerchief and blew her nose and dabbed at her eyes. ÊMy point is╔ what I originally said╔ is that family ties are quite tricky. ÊYouıll have to forgive me, dear, but itıs ever so hard not to still think of you all as children.Ë Hermioneıs resolve completely broke then, and she shot out of her chair to hug the older witch tightly. ÊDoes he make you happy?Ë she asked Hermione softly once they parted. ÊVery.Ë ÊTreats you with respect?Ë ÊHe does.Ë ÊTakes you on outlandish dates?Ë ÊHeıs whisking me away to France all next week.Ë ÊGood,Ë said Molly and wiped her eyes dry. If you pop out the side door, youıll find him.Ë Gesturing with an encouraging smile, Molly pointed her to the exit. I am going to ask you to simply listen to what I have to say.Ë Arthur sighed then, and Hermione felt in that moment he looked older than sheıd ever seen him; a man whoıd raised seven children and lived through two wars. ÊMy personal feelings for Lucius aside, I feel it is my duty to caution you about what getting involved with a family like that could mean.Ë ÊDraco is not his father,Ë Hermione asserted sternly. Arthur held up a placating hand. ÊIım not accusing Draco of anything. I simply want to stress that there is a certain burden that comes with the Malfoy name, warranted or not, and I would hate for you to have to suffer unduly. Pureblood family traditions being what they are, should you and Draco declare anything publicly, certain expectations could be thrust upon you, not to mention the media frenzy this would incite.Ë Hermione pursed her lips, heart clenching in her chest. She knew what Arthur meant to caution her against, and while she appreciated he cared enough to warn her, part of her boiled in indignation at the publicıs perception of her, of Draco, and of the moronic blood-based hierarchy that seemed to be the root of all the problems in the wizarding world. Why couldnıt everyone just bloody move on already? When she met Arthurıs eyes again, he smiled wryly as if he knew exactly what she had been thinking. She handed it to a puzzled Arthur. ÊHereıs something I think you should read. Then, steeling herself for the discussion she now needed to have with her biological parents, she disapparated. He felt like heıd been run over by the Hogwarts Express. If Draco had the energy to feel anything besides bone-deep exhaustion and melancholy, he would have been mortified. Sure, heıd had a few minor night terrors in Hermioneıs presence before, but nothing on this scale. Merlin, heıd thrown up in front of her. Disgusting. She, of course, had handled it all in stride. Somehow between his motherıs untimely arrival, Hermioneıs breakdown at the Potters, and Draco retching all over her floorboards heıd been unable to summon those three little words. It hadnıt been quite the romantic evening heıd envisioned. But oh, heıd been so very close. When she pinned him to the spot with those overlarge eyes of hers and asked why heıd done all this work on her behalf, it almost tumbled out. When sheıd chased away his nightmares and made him feel safe under her healing touch, it almost lurched from his throat. Fuck, but he was pathetic. Would it have been rather audacious of him to tell her though? Honestly, the nerve of him, thinking he had a right to say those words after what he dreamed about his family doing to her. Not just a dream, he thought bitterly, a real memory. Back in November when Draco had begged her forgiveness for his non-interference as she suffered under his auntıs knife and wand, Hermione had said she wanted to discuss that night, eventually, when they were both ready. Perhaps it was best heıd held his tongue until theyıd well and truly come to terms with that awful night at Malfoy Manor. I am okay with this. Draco rubbed his tired eyes and set aside the parchment for the fresh ink to dry. Heıd finished drafting the request to Bellamy for the time off the following week, pleased that he would only be missing practices and a single match. Heıd also already sent off correspondence to his solicitors letting them know heıd be out of the country as of Friday evening and used Hermioneıs Floo to make the proper reservations at his preferred hotel in Paris. Galleons were no object for this holiday (or any holiday, for that matter) and apparently throwing enough of them around combined with his surname was enough to secure the exact suite he desired. He desperately needed a shower. Dragging his exhausted body to Hermioneıs bathroom, he let the shower heat up until the water could almost be classified as scalding before getting in under the spray. He closed his eyes and some of the tension had finally left his muscles when he heard itÐthe voice he hadnıt heard in years. Oh Draco, what has become of you? Draco jolted, and his eyes popped open at the sneering drawl that seemed to echo all around him. You love her, do you? Honestly Draco, the Mudblood? ÊShut up,Ë Draco snarled aloud. ÊShut up, shut up, shut up.Ë He tried to throw himself into his occlumency, but his deceased fatherıs voice would not be silenced. Itıs pathetic, pining over such filth. Do you really think she could ever return your affections? How could you let yourself be so weak? Draco slapped his palms against the shower wall. ÊEnough!Ë He was arguing with a ghost, with a figment of his deranged imagination. Her kind is deceitful. Donıt you see how she has fooled the world around her into believing she is some sort of powerful witch, to be revered and adored? Sheıs nothing but a common, dirty Muggle, same as her parents. Draco growled and shut off the water. He pressed his fingers against his temples and tried to massage his growing headache away, determined to ignore the insidious voice of his subconscious. Fooled you too, hasnıt she? But you remember, son, donıt you? How she begged and screamed for mercy under your auntıs wand like a pathetic animal? Draco stumbled out of the shower, held himself up against the sink, and glared into the mirror still fogged with steam from the hot water. In the misted reflection, he thought for a moment he could see another presence in the bathroom; a visage of white-blond hair and gray eyes too startlingly similar to his own for comfort. Youıre a fool, Draco. Pretend all you like that youıre a better man than me. But we both know the truth. Draco let out a scream of fury and slammed his closed fist into the mirror, cracking the glass. ÊYouıre wrong,Ë he whispered brokenly. ÊAnd youıre dead.Ë A ringing silence greeted his statement as he staggered back from the counter and cradled his bloody knuckles. A movement in the corner of his eye startled him, and he turned to see Crookshanks watching him beadily from the doorway. ÊMade a bit of a mess, havenıt we? If youıre quite through with your tantrum, youıd best clean this up before she gets back.Ë Draco frowned at the animal but retrieved his wand and in two flicks had repaired both the bathroom mirror and his bloodied knuckles, though some bruises remained. Crookshanks let out a sniff of approval and then sauntered off, leaving Draco alone to berate himself for falling prey to the haunting apparition his mind had created. Salazar, this holiday with Hermione could not come soon enough if hearing the lilting taunts of Lucius in his head rendered him mad enough to punch a mirror with his bare hand. As he emerged dressed from the bedroom, determined to finally master Hermioneıs Muggle coffee machine, the Floo lit up and a familiar red-head stepped through. ÊAfternoon, Malfoy.Ë ÊWeasley.Ë ÊItıs Potter.Ë ÊWhatever.Ë Ginny regarded him thoughtfully for a moment and Draco rolled his eyes. He didnıt need or want any gestures of sympathy from her. ÊWell? What do you want? Granger isnıt here. In fact, sheıs supposedly at the Nest or Pigpen or whatever your oversized brood calls your home.Ë ÊItıs the Burrow,Ë Ginny replied calmly, not taking his bait. ÊAnd she is there, but I asked her if I could come speak with you while she finishes up and heads to her parents. Couldnıt make us a cuppa?Ë ÊDo I look like a bloody house-elf?Ë he sneered. ÊNo, you look like youıve been trampled by a herd of hippogriff.Ë Draco turned on his heel and strode into the kitchen. ÊIım fixing myself a pot of coffee. You can either accept that or make your own damn tea.Ë Ginny followed and plopped down into a chair at the kitchen table, making herself quite at home. ÊThanks ever so, my most gracious host!Ë Draco scowled and made no response as he fiddled with the Muggle coffee maker. Surely this wasnıt that difficult if it required no magic? As if she could sense his dilemma, Ginny appeared at his side. ÊOh you are a pathetic one, arenıt you? Iıll do it this once, you prat, Harry and I have one of these at home.Ë Draco folded his arms and stalked over to the table, throwing himself down in her vacated seat.
