The diagnostic spells notified me that you were awake. Iım Healer Benson and this isÐË ÊWhere is she?Ë Draco cut off whatever introductory tripe would fall next out of the healerıs mouth. The healer pursed his lips into a frown. ÊIım afraid I cannotÐË Draco leapt out of bed and drew himself up to his fullest height. ÊWhere is she?Ë he thundered in the manıs face. Out of the corner of his eye, Draco clocked the other healer removing her wand from a holster at her side. ÊI am not at liberty to give you details of other patients. Now as for youÐË ÊWHERE THE FUCK IS SHE? TELL ME RIGHT NOW OR I SWEAR I WILLÐ!Ë ÊMr. Malfoy if you do not calm down I will have no choice but to have you physically restrained!Ë Healer Benson fired back, completely unfazed by Dracoıs yelling and proximity. The wand twitched in the other healerıs hand. Draco backed away and sank down on the edge of his bed, glaring mutinously at the two healers in front of him. He felt Healer Browningıs presence at his back. If the old man even thought about placing a comforting hand on his shoulder, Draco would rip it off. ÊYouıve survived an intense Dementor attack,Ë continued Healer Benson in a bored, clinical voice, as if Draco had not screamed in his face moments ago. ÊYour magical reserves were heavily depleted by your casting of a Patronus and subsequent Warming Charms and then Apparition. The French healers transferred you here almost immediately and you have been magically sedated for 24 hours. We also treated you for hypothermia, dehydration, and mild frostbite to your lips and ears. Your magic has made a full recovery, as have your extremities. It is my professional advice that you stay at least another 6 hours to fully heal physically and ÐË ÊDischarge me now,Ë Draco interrupted. ÊDraco, I donıt thinkÐË began Healer Browning, but Draco cut him off. ÊNow. Discharge me.Ë The female healer who hadnıt bothered introducing herself conjured a form and a quill. She thrust it at him with a terse, ÊYou can sign this saying youıre requesting a discharge against your Healerıs advice.Ë Draco signed it without reading and threw it back at her. ÊFantastic,Ë he clipped. ÊWhere might I find Hermione Granger?Ë Both healers stared back impassively, but Draco heard a quick intake of breath from Browning behind him. ÊAs I said before, I cannot give you private details regarding other patients.Ë ÊIs she alive? Is she all right? Just take me to her, please, IÐË ÊMr. Malfoy as you are neither immediate family nor listed as next of kin, you are not entitled to any information regarding Ms. Granger.Ë So she was alive then, or at least, still alive. And she was here, somewhere in the hospital. Dracoıs blood boiled at the two adversaries in front of him, who dared reduce his relationship with Hermione to nothing. He could perhaps throw his weight around using his donor status and threats of lawsuits, but a better plan suddenly surfaced in his mind. He had a card up his sleeve that these two idiots had no idea he possessed, and Draco planned to lay it down the second they left the room. ÊFine,Ë Draco said coolly. ÊThanks ever so much for your help. Such a pleasure to see my many Galleons at work in this institution,Ë he sneered. The healers left without another word, though Draco knew it almost killed the witch to not roll her eyes at him. The second the door shut, Draco snatched his wand up. He had to focus if he wanted to do this correctly. ÊThey told me you arrived with a young woman. That you had both experienced a Dementor attack and suffered from exposure, but that you were able to apparate both of you to safety,Ë Browning said numbly. ÊDraco the woman you were with╔ why didnıt you tell me you were dating Hermione Granger?Ë Draco paused his concentration to glare at his healer. ÊWhat difference does it make? You know all about her from my sessions, I wasnıt aware I had to disclose her full name. Do you need her address and date of birth as well?Ë he said coldly. ÊNormally, no, I would not need personal details of a romantic partner, but Draco this is quite the special circumstance given your history and past healing sessions! Donıt you think I would have been able to tailor our sessions differently if youıd only been honest? This isnıt some trivial detail about your love life, this is a major revelation that is necessary for me to treat you properly!Ë Draco had never seen Browning angry with him before. It would have had more of an impact if Draco had the time or inclination to care about anything other than Hermione. Ignoring his healerıs outrage at his perceived duplicity, Draco closed his eyes to focus on his happiest memory. Or fantasy, he supposed. He needed to research Patronuses and how they were specifically conjured. A white dress, a wide smile. ÊI Hermione, take you, Draco╔Ë ÊExpecto Patronum!Ë The silvery dragon emerged and floated in front of him, awaiting orders. ÊTake this message to Harry Potter: ÈPotter, itıs Malfoy. Iım at St. Mungoıs and they have Granger here somewhere but the fucking sods wonıt tell me where, so I need you to get your arse down here immediately and pull that Savior of All that is Good and Just in the World thing you do and make sure sheıs all right and take me to her.ıË There. Let the idiotic staff here refuse a request from Harry Potter. The dragon flew from the room, and Draco met Browningıs stupefied look. ÊIım going to find her, you can save your admonitions for our next session,Ë he said flatly then shucked the standard issue patient gown and pulled on his own clothes. ÊIım scheduling an additional 30 minutes for our next session,Ë said Browning. ÊWeıll be discussing why youıve listed me as next of kin in addition to your reasons for not discussing your relationship with Ms. Granger.Ë ÊWhatever,Ë Draco muttered and looked up quickly as the ward door opened. ÊThat was fast,Ë Draco said as a harried Potter strode over to him. ÊWhat was?Ë came the blank reply. ÊDidnıt you get my Patronus? How are you already here?Ë Suddenly, the misty dragon appeared and delivered Dracoıs message. Harry smirked as the Patronus faded away. ÊImpressive. Although I am disappointed itıs not a ferret.Ë Draco bristled at the insult, but shoved his pride down. ÊPotter, you have to help me find her. The healers wouldnıt tell me a thing and I donıt even know if sheıs all right and Iıve no way of getting to her and I need to know if sheÐ!Ë Harry held up a hand to stop Dracoıs anxious babbling. ÊMalfoy, sheıs fine. If you can calm down Iıll take you to her now.Ë A cough behind him reminded Draco they werenıt alone. ÊDraco we still should discussÐË ÊNot today, Browning,Ë Draco snapped. ÊBrowning this is the Boy Who Doesnıt Know How to Die nor Comb His Hair. Potter, this is my personal healer, Browning.Ë Draco rolled his eyes as the two politely shook hands. ÊHarry Potter, pleasure to meet you.Ë ÊAtticus Browning, likewise.Ë As he hurried after Potter out of the ward, Draco swore he heard Browning mutter under his breath. ÊShould have just bloody retired like Mariana asked, Iım far too old for this╔Ë The two men stalked down the hall together, Draco peppering Harry with questions about Hermioneıs wellbeing. The questions went ignored until they reached a deserted corridor. Ê╔and her lips were sort of blue, and Iıve no idea what state I arrived in, but I think she was worse off than me, but if they administered the right potion straight away, then there shouldnıt be any lasting╔Ë Harry cut him off mid-rant. ÊMerlin, will you shut up? I canıt give you any answers until I know weıre alone!Ë Draco clamped his mouth shut, but glared. ÊSheıs fine. Hermioneıs fine.Ë Harry repeated and finally the words sank in. Draco fell heavily against the wall and covered his face in his hands. All the anger and adrenaline at needing information bled away as the crush of relief and worry washed over him. ÊMalfoy,Ë said Potter quietly. ÊSheıs alive thanks to you. You╔ you saved her life.Ë Potterıs tight voice only made Draco feel worse. How close had he been to losing her? Gods, what would have happened if theyıd been out there just a minute more? Or if he never succeeded in casting the Patronus? Potterıs reaction made it seem as if heıd been close to grief. How long had Hermione hovered between this world and the next? Had she suffered, again, because of him? Harry leaned on his side against the wall, facing Draco and crossing his arms. ÊAre you all right?Ë Under normal circumstances, he would have bit out a scathing reply and told Potter to sod off, but the fight had left him. Draco dropped his hands and drew in a shaky breath. ÊI can see her?Ë he asked softly. Harry nodded and pushed his glasses up his nose. ÊSheıs sleeping now, but yes, this is her room.Ë Harry gestured to the door in front of them. ÊShe asked for you first thing,Ë he added. ÊRon and Ginny are with her now.Ë Draco squeezed his eyes shut, willing the terrible images of her frozen in the woods to disappear. ÊHer parents? Do they know?Ë ÊAhh, no.Ë Harry hedged. ÊMe and Ron╔ weıre listed as her next of kin here. Her parents donıt╔ well Iım sure you know, itıs a bit difficult between all of them. Hermione doesnıt like them to worry. You were still supposed to be in France for another two days anyhow.Ë Draco huffed out a bitter laugh. ÊAnd if sheıd died?Ë Harryıs mouth set in a grim line. ÊShe didnıt die. Come on.Ë Draco pushed off the wall and followed Harry into another private ward. Yet instead of the reassurance he hoped for, Draco saw his worst fear laid out before him. He saw Hermione in a bed too big for her small frame, her hair flowing wildly all around her delicate face, eyes closed and with skin so pale. She was too still, too quiet. Was she even breathing? She looked like a shell of herself╔ like a corpse. Like a limp little doll being tortured on his drawing room floor╔ Dracoıs vision blurred and he swayed on his feet. Distantly he heard someone calling his name but all sound was muffled to his ears. At the edge of his swimming eyes he could see two moving shapes with red hair, but he couldnıt look away from the pale, lifeless imitation of the woman he loved lying prone, never to wake. He was too late, always too late. He couldnıt save her, he never saved her╔ A concerned face came into view, and Draco realized he only remained standing because Potter held him steady. He said something in an urgent tone, but Draco couldnıt make out the words. ÊI╔ canıt╔ no. I canıt do this╔ IÐË Draco gasped and wrenched himself away and bolted out of the ward. He couldnıt breathe. He needed to be able to just fucking breathe. She was so deathly pale. As pale as when the Snatchers paraded her into the Manor. Draco staggered down the hall and into the nearest washroom and all but collapsed over the sink. He gripped the back of his head tightly and choked on a sob. Eventually his misery took its physical toll and he retched into the basin. He dry heaved a few more times before his body gave up trying to expel whatever healing potions heıd been dosed with earlier. I am okay with this I am okay with this I am okay with this. Draco wiped his face clean and gulped down some water, then took a good look at the ghastly visage in the mirror. Potter had claimed sheıd recovered but how could he say that when she looked so fragile, so broken? A sudden movement behind him caught Dracoıs attention and then he saw another face reflected over his shoulder. He couldnıt help the sense of dÄjê vu. ÊWe must stop meeting like this, Potter,Ë Dracoıs weak voice attempted a drawl. Harryıs lips quirked briefly. ÊI promise not to try out experimental dark curses that make you bleed out on the floor this time.Ë Draco shrugged but couldnıt suppress the trembling in his weakened limbs. The memory of that incident hit him in full force: the helplessness, the desperation, the abject misery he had felt╔ ÊI wanted to die that day, you know. Donıt get me wrong, I sought to hurt you, but when you hit me with that curse and I started bleeding╔ I thought Iıd finally found my way out of my fucked up situation. It had finally gotten through my thick head that I was doomed to fail the Dark Lordıs mission from the start. And what do you know?Ë Draco laughed darkly. ÊI even failed at dying.Ë Draco closed his eyes so he didnıt have to see the concern etched on Potterıs face; the same that day in their Sixth Year, too, when theyıd locked eyes in the cracked mirror. Potterıs pity had infuriated him then, but now he could only muster a muted disgust. ÊYou had another way, you always did,Ë Potter said quietly. ÊI realize itıs easy for me to say that. I only wish that╔ that Dumbledore╔or anyone could have shown you much sooner that you did have a choice.Ë Draco barked out a harsh laugh. ÊA choice? Thatıs rich,Ë he sneered and turned to lean back on the wall. Why were they even having this pointless discussion? But Draco found that now theyıd gone down this road, he might as well see it to the end. ÊYou're the Boy Who Lived, yes? Consider me the Boy With No Choice.Ë Draco sank down the wall so he could sit with his knees propped up. ÊThanks to my fatherıs blunders, I was made to fulfill certain terrifying expectations just as Iıd turned 16. Perhaps younger me would assert he welcomed it at first, but we both know my choice was between taking the Mark and certain death. Would you call that a choice at all? And as for my mission to kill Dumbledore╔ kill the old man or watch your parents die. Which path would you choose?Ë Draco looked up at Harry thoughtfully. ÊI heard a rumor once that the Sorting Hat considered you for Slytherin, had a whole conversation with you over it. Did you know it said nothing to me? Remember our Sorting Ceremony? It barely grazed my hair and declared me Slytherin. Didnıt offer me any glimpses of alternate paths, no words of wisdom over how my life might turn out differently, no, it simply swept me along my predetermined path of darkness.Ë Harry leveled him with a patient stare. ÊI told you this once before, but I think it bears repeating. The world is not split into good people and Death Eaters.Ë Draco tipped his head back and trained his eyes on the ceiling. ÊYes, yes, very pretty words, those.Ë Though he didnıt look up to verify, Draco would have bet a lot of gold that Potter rolled his eyes. ÊLook, Iım not here to rehash the past. You apologized before, I forgave you, weıre beyond that now. Hell, youıre dating my best friend and rescuing her from Dementors! I came here to thank you and make sure you werenıt punishing yourself. It wasnıt your fault and the healers and Hermione told us what you did for her.Ë Draco cringed and hung his head. What heıd done for her╔ it was laughable. What heıd done was get her into yet another dangerous situation that almost got her killed. ÊShe almost died. She╔Ë To his utter mortification, Draco felt his throat close up. ÊMalfoy,Ë Potter sighed exasperatedly, Êwhat part of ÈHermioneıs fineı did you not understand?Ë ÊOh? He closed his eyes so as to block out the sight of the too-relaxed form of his former rival, standing casual as-you-please against a bathroom door while Draco divulged his deepest secret. ÊAnd I never told her... she almost died and I never told her,Ë Draco whispered hoarsely. "Why not?" Dracoıs eyes snapped open to glare up at the bane of his adolescence. "In case you haven't noticed, Auror Potter, things don't quite work out when I have something to lose,Ë he snarled. Potter let out another long-suffering sigh and if Draco had more energy he would have hexed him between the eyes. ÊLook, you really need to quit being so dramatic, and thatıs saying a lot coming from me.Ë He shot Draco a roguish grin that made bile rise in his throat again. ÊWarıs over, mate. Been over a long time now. The only way you stand to lose Hermione now is if you continue to act like a twatÐË ÊI almost lost her to Dementors, so donıt you dare tell meÐË ÊYes, a group of rogue Dementors, I got the full story from the French Aurors. Apparently itıs been a problem in parts of Europe since we banished them from Azkaban. But you fought them off, Malfoy.Ë Green eyes bore into gray. ÊYou fought for her. And Iım telling you right now that if she wakes up again and youıre not there this time? Dementors will seem like Pygmy Puffs compared to her.Ë Draco indulged in a wry half-grin, knowing full well what a furious Hermione could inflict upon those who upset her. And while she would have every right to be angry with him, it would never take away from the fact that he didnıt deserve her. He, the idiot having a meltdown on a filthy hospital bathroom floor, who was too much of a coward to sit at her bedside and wait for her to wake, could never deserve her. Which meant he desperately needed to quash his silly little fantasy. His thrilling, ridiculous, impossible, and absolutely terrifying fantasy. Yes, terrifying. Terrifying that his brain had even thought to travel anywhere near that sort of future. He couldnıt even summon the words to tell Hermione he loved her and yet his mind in a panicked state saw fit to conjure a vision of her agreeing to be bound to him, heart, soul, and magical core, forever. Had Draco really been that blind to the depth of his own feelings? Madness. Sheer madness. As if sheıd ever agree to tie herself to someone like him. Marriage. Forever. Why and how was that the source of enough happiness for Draco to finally cast a corporeal Patronus? Where the fuck had that come from? With a stab of misery, he realized it didnıt matter anyway. Draco couldnıt be the man in his own fantasy; a man worthy of the glowing smile of that remarkable woman agreeing to be his wife. Heıd never be that man. ÊI canıt do this╔ I canıt be what she needs. I canıtÐË ÊBollocks to the ÈI canıt!ı What you can do is bloody try! You can grow the fuck up and at least try for her! Pull your head out of your poncey arse and be the man who was strong enough to ward off Dementors and carry her two miles in the snow! Whatıs the alternative, eh? Make yourself miserable and make her miserable because youıre too pig-headed to see she loves you too? Hermione doesnıt do anything by halves, so my advice is to count yourself as one lucky sod and thank Merlin every day you get to be in her presence.Ë Potterıs chest heaved by the end of his little tirade, and this more than anything succeeded in buoying Draco. Now this was familiar ground! An irate Harry Potter, raining self-righteousness down upon him. Draco stood up slowly and ran a hand through his hair, settling it back in place. He rubbed at his nearly dry eyes and straightened his clothes. I am okay with this. ÊIf that is your version of a supportive lecture Potter, I pity your future child. You probably shouldnıt curse so much, itıs unbecoming of a father, you know.Ë Harry snorted and muttered something derogatory under his breath. The two men regarded each other and shared understanding grimaces; not exactly a handshake, but a mutual acknowledgment of a commonality all the same. Harry jerked his head toward the washroom door. ÊCome on, then. Gin and I have to be off and Ron promised to inform the rest of his family about her condition. You can stay with her now, no one will bother you over it.Ë Draco said nothing, even if he was begrudgingly grateful several times over to Potter today. Just before they reached the head of the ward, Draco asked the question that still plagued him. ÊPotter╔ when you cast a Patronus╔ you have to use a memory right? Something thatıs already happened?Ë Harry shrugged. ÊWell, yes, thatıs the technique I was taught and it always works for me. Hermione would know better if there are any alternate theories out there. Obviously it has to be something that inspires happiness. Not just typical happy in a way thatıs fleeting. More like╔ more like╔ a memory that makes you so happy that if it were the last thing you experienced it would be worth it.Ë A white dress, a wide smile. ÊI Hermione, take you, Draco╔Ë Harry gave him a searching look. ÊWhy? What did you think about when you cast it?Ë Draco didnıt answer, just determinedly entered Hermioneıs room and focused on putting one foot in front of the other as he approached her bed. He didnıt spare a glance at the two other figures in the room, either. He sank into the chair and tried not to retch again at the sight of her looking so frail. Her chest rose and fell gently, and only by timing his own breathing with the movement did he feel himself begin to calm. ÊWhen she wakes, tell Hermione Iıll Floo her tomorrow,Ë Potterıs voice called softly from the doorway. He must have also gestured to the Weasleys in the room, because he could hear footsteps shuffling past. Draco did not care to address them, he only had eyes for Hermione, and so it caught him by surprise when a small, warm hand squeezed his shoulder in what one might consider affection. He looked up into the fierce brown eyes of Ginny, who regarded him with a tremulous smile. Thank you, she mouthed, and he merely blinked back at her. She didnıt seem to expect any other reaction from him and dropped her hand to follow the others out. Before the door closed, another voice called out to him. ÊMalfoy.Ë Draco turned in the chair to see Ron Weasley hovering at the threshold. The red-head sported a pained sort of frown, but he looked Draco in the eye. ÊThanks,Ë his eyes flicked to Hermione then back to Draco. ÊFor what you did╔ for her. Just╔ thanks.Ë Draco nodded once and turned away. He didnıt have the emotional or physical energy to unpack his feelings toward the Weasel just now. When he heard the door swing shut for good, he gave into his urge and wrapped Hermioneıs hand in his own. Based on appearance, he expected it to be cold to the touch, but she felt as warm and comforting as always. The familiar feel of her skin made him choke on an emotional exhale. Heıd never let her out of his sight again. Relief weighed heavily on a tired Draco, forcing him to sink into the stiff chair. He kept his grip on Hermione, even as exhaustion staked its claim on him. Never letting you go again, Granger. I love you. I am okay with this. Chapter End Notes A/N: Thank you so much for reading and continuing on, you are all incredible for supporting this story. ÊIf the end of your sentence isnıt going to be Èwonderfully supportive boyfriendı or Èawfully brave man for saving your lifeı then I think you best not say anything at all,Ë she said coldly and it took all of Dracoıs muscle control not to smirk. Home as well?Ë Draco could picture the arching of Hermioneıs eyebrow. ÊYou know which breed that is, donıt you?Ë Draco shook his head, having only ever seen dragons during the first task of the Triwizard Tournament in his Fourth Year, and only then from a great distance. ÊA Ukrainian Ironbelly, the largest breed of dragon with scales of a metallic gray╔ itıs the same breed we freed from Gringotts.Ë And me, Granger. You freed me, too. ÊWhat did you think of to conjure it?Ë Draco clenched his jaw, knowing he would have to skirt around the truth. He could tell her now, but was a hospital really the right setting for such a declaration? Hardly, he reasoned, and contented himself with how she felt in his arms. ÊSomething that made me╔ happier than Iıve ever been.Ë Certainly not a lie, but not even close to the whole story. Though Hermione furrowed her brow at his dodge, she let the matter drop and settled back against him. ÊMalfoy! Did you replace my bed sheets?Ë Draco froze in the kitchen, in the process of helping himself to some tea. ÊWhy? Are you allergic to silk?Ë Hermione stomped into view, hands on her hips. ÊDonıt you dare try to smooth talk your way out of this. You had no right to meddle in my things without my consent!Ë ÊHark whoıs talking, donıt think I havenıt noticed that youıve swapped out all my toothpaste for that spearmint Muggle stuff!Ë Her glare crumbled into a sheepish smile. ÊFine, weıll call it even then.Ë She turned on her heel to see to the rest of her unpacking from their trip. ÊItıs all right to admit youıve developed a taste for the finer things in life, Granger,Ë he teased and quickly downed his tea. Theyıd been out of St. Mungoıs for a few hours and Draco still put off returning to his own home. Now go sit on the couch, Potterıs Floo-calling you in 10 minutes.Ë He stuck his tongue out when she pouted and stepped into the fire. When he stepped through his own grate into the traveling parlor he was met with another scowling witch. ÊHave a lovely stay at St. Mungoıs did you?Ë Narcissaıs shrill voice tore through the room as she hurried to Draco and threw a letter at him. ÊA copy of your discharge notice arrived this morning. I should have been alerted immediately, I should have been called to your bedside and ensured you were properly cared for! What if you had died? What ifÐ?Ë Her voice wavered but did not quite break. ÊHermioneıs fine, thank you for asking.Ë He bit out and his mother pursed her lips. ÊShe was worse off than me, if you cared to know, so I stayed with her in hospital and made sure she settled in all right at home. In fact,Ë Draco walked past her and moved toward the stairs, ÊIım only here to pick up a few things and return to her.Ë ÊDraco!Ë Narcissa called and he froze on the bottom step. ÊYou are quite serious about her?Ë He regarded his mother carefully and sighed, ÊVery.Ë Narcissaıs head tilted from side to side, an idea sliding around her mind. ÊIıd like to meet her properly, then. Would you be opposed to dinner at my home once the renovations are complete?Ë ÊThat sounds agreeable, Iıll ask her. Draco stared after her for a beat, wondering when things began to feel so suffocating whenever they were in the same room for too long. Draco still remembered the time in his life when his parents were the center of his whole universe. Was this strain a normal evolution of the parental-child relationship as one became an adult? With a heavy mind and heart, Draco gathered a few essentials and Flooed back to Hermione. ÊYou just missed Harry,Ë Hermione said from her spot on the couch. ÊYou say that like I wanted to see his face.Ë She narrowed her eyes at him. ÊMmhmm. Has been ever since Ginny returned him. Itıs the same after every time I go away for more than two days.Ë Hermione snuggled into Dracoıs side and sighed contentedly. ÊI spoke to Ron, too,Ë she said softly. ÊHe apologized╔ for the way he spoke to me about you╔ and for the way he spoke to me in general. I think heıs still trying to come to terms with╔ us. But he is trying. ÊDo that then! Iım not some weak little woman who needs you to take care of her becauseÐ!Ë ÊYouıre missing the point!Ë he barked and stood to tower over her. ÊMy entire life Iıve never been capable of taking care of you so let me do this at least!Ë ÊIım not asking you to!Ë ÊMaybe I feel like I have to!Ë ÊYou donıt have to prove anything to me Draco!Ë ÊI DO!Ë he shouted and Hermioneıs mouth clamped shut at both the noise level of his response as well as the vehemence behind it. ÊDonıt you think I know exactly what made you fall to that Dementor? Hermione deflated instantly and shook her head. ÊYou did not do anything to me. Bellatrix was the one who tortured me, not you. You didnıt do anything ðË ÊExactly!Ë he cried. ÊI didnıt do anything and you almost fucking died, right there at my feet.Ë He ran a hand down his face. ÊItıs eating at me, Granger, I canıt stand it. You probably have so many awful memories of me╔ memories that cause you nothing but pain.Ë ÊNot anymore,Ë she said softly and placed a hand on his arm. ÊAnd even before that, Iıll tell you again what I told you before our trip. Youıre a good man, Draco.Ë Draco closed his eyes. He could no longer bear to look at her while she said these things. ÊI wish that were enough,Ë he replied. Hermione removed her hand from his face to grasp at his fingers. ÊCome on, come lay in bed with me, we need to talk.Ë He followed her obediently and laid down on his side, facing her. ÊDo you remember, way back in November, what I said to you after our dinner? After you apologized to me?Ë ÊSome of it.Ë ÊI said that one day, when we were both ready, I would want to talk about that night the Snatchers brought me, Harry, and Ron to Malfoy Manor. ÊAll right.Ë ÊDo you want a Calming Draught?Ë Sweet Salazar, did this witchıs compassion know no bounds? I love you. ÊNo I╔ I can do this,Ë he asserted. ÊI think,Ë Hermione took a deep breath, ÊI think we need to start further back. No judgment, no scorn, just listening as they each unraveled the separate stories of their lives. They traded off memories, the good and the bad, and instead of feeling like an extra healing session with Browning, Draco found he didnıt mind sharing these long-buried anecdotes with Hermione. Draco told her about growing up at Malfoy Manor. It was a strict, yet charmed life. He told her about the many tutors, his every hour scheduled down to the minute with unending lessons on etiquette, history (wizarding and familial), French, Latin, flying, swimming, waltzing, piano, geography, politics, runes, arithmancy, reading/writing, and eventually spellcasting and potions. He wanted for nothing, obviously, his parents indulged his every whim, urge, and tantrum. Yes, heavy expectations were upon his head (sole heir and all) but there was never a doubt in Dracoıs mind that his parents loved him. Hermione told him about growing up in a posh suburb. It was a cloistered, yet charmed life. She told him about her proud parents, so pleased their daughter showed such an affinity for reading and learning at a young age. Hermione was clearly a gifted child, and they treated her as such. She too had many extracurricular lessons outside of primary school, and a clear expectation that she would one day assume responsibility over her parentsı dental practice. She wanted for nothing, and but for her Hogwarts letter would have had her pick of any number of upper-class secondary schools. There was never a doubt in her mind that her parents loved her. Dracoıs Hogwarts letter arrived and it was everything for which heıd been prepared. His parents assured him heıd be a prince among boys, and then when Draco came of age, a king among men. Hermioneıs Hogwarts letter arrived and it upended her familyıs entire life. Her parents assured her sheıd go to this special school and be just as brilliant as if sheıd gone to a Muggle school. Draco arrived at Hogwarts and didnıt have any friends. He had admirers, minions, and sycophants eager to exploit a connection with the young Malfoy heir. Hermione arrived at Hogwarts and didnıt have any friends. She didnıt know a single soul, had no family legacy to uphold, and was so keen to prove herself worthy of her own magic that she didnıt put herself out there unless it involved imparting facts. It was all fun and games for Draco (taunting Potter, ruling over Slytherin, preparing for his future as an aristocratic kingmaker) for the first five years. Then the time came for him to put away childish things and schoolyard grudges. He was to become a servant for the Dark. It was all breathtaking adventures and exhilarating impossibilities for Hermione (saving the Sorcererıs Stone, rescuing Sirius and Buckbeak, helping Harry with the Triwizard Tournament) for the first four years. Then the time came for action, preparation, and danger unlike sheıd ever known before. She was to become a warrior for the Light. When Draco had reached the point in his story about his initiation before the Dark Lord, he faltered. Hermione, no doubt recognizing his hesitation and the emotion behind it, took his hand and interlaced their fingers. ÊI wanted it,Ë he murmured hoarsely. ÊI wanted that damned Mark on my skin. Father had screwed up and this was my chance to prove to everyone that I could be the one to bring glory to my familyıs name. An honor, I thought, foolishly.Ë He pushed out a harsh laugh. ÊGods, I was naòve. I thought I was so important. I was chosen. Me, so young and given such a vital mission. What a fucking joke.Ë Draco met her eyes, expecting to see disgust, anger, or some form of chastisement (really, Malfoy what were you thinking?). But he saw none of that. ÊMy life had been so easy and I thought this would be just the same. That others would do all the dirty work for me and Iıd sit firmly at the top of the food chain, reaping the benefits of a pureblood society.Ë By the time heıd realized how in over his head he was, he confessed to Hermione, it was far too late. He told her about his wretched Sixth Year: he couldnıt eat, couldnıt sleep, couldnıt concentrate in classes, obsessed with fixing that cursed Vanishing Cabinet and somehow sparing his parentsı lives from the Dark Lordıs wrath. Moaning Myrtle his only confidant: the ghost of a Muggleborn girl who could no more help him than come back to life. Then came that horrible night on the Astronomy Tower. But when face to face with his intended victim, he couldnıt say the spell. Dumbledore acted calm and merciful to the last and Draco spent countless hours after that fateful night fantasizing about what might have happened had he accepted clemency. In Dracoıs healing sessions, Browning advised against playing the hindsight game, but it was damn near impossible to avoid. ÊI should tell you,Ë Draco suddenly interrupted his own story. Looking at her for this next part might be his undoing, so he kept his eyes up and continued his tale of woe. The Dark Lord lived in his family home, if you could call it that anymore. The Malfoys were a laughingstock amongst the Death Eater ranks by this time. His father, emasculated and discarded, a wandless failure. His mother, ignored, a socialite who could bring nothing of strategic value to the table. And then Draco, the gutless schoolboy. The Dark Lord enjoyed having Draco dole out torture sessions to fellow Death Eaters who botched missions, Muggle and Muggleborn victims, and anyone kept captive in the Manorıs cellar. But Draco clearly didnıt have the stomach for it and the Dark Lord soon grew bored of needling the Malfoy boy and busied himself with more important matters, leaving the control of the Manor to Bellatrix. Draco had to return to Hogwarts anyway, a slight reprieve for him. Snape and the Carrows left him alone, seeing as they were all part of the same little cult, so Draco existed in a sort of mindless daze, wondering when it would all end, and how. And then, the Easter holidays. ÊI thought for certain that this time╔ this time I was going to die. Hopeless. Hermione reached up to brush some of his hair off his forehead and then leaned in for a brief kiss. ÊHow are you feeling? That was a lot,Ë she said with a quiet chuckle. ÊLike a wrung sponge,Ë he said bluntly. Hermione pressed another kiss to his lips with a ghost of an apology written on her face. ÊItıs late, we should try and sleep.Ë Instead, Draco watched as she drifted off, content to merely hold her. Never again, he vowed to himself. Never again would he be the scared boy in the corner, wand held limply at his side. Checking to see sheıd fallen asleep, Draco rolled up the left sleeve of his shirt to his elbow. Just a skull and a snake now, and barely recognizable as such, especially if you werenıt of magical origin. Nothing but faded ink and grotesque, to be sure, but at least devoid of dark magic. And even with the cursed magic that once resided in the shape long gone, the smudge remained, not removable by any methods heıd attempted. Heıd stopped trying long ago. He glared at the blurry, grayish symbol, wishing for perhaps the hundredth time that he were anyone else, any other nameless man with a clean forearm and unburdened history. Never again. But wishes of that nature were futile, self-defeating, and he knew Hermione would tear him a new one should she ever develop the skill for Legilimency. Heıd earned the trust of the witch slumbering in his arms and that thought served to collect the shattered fragments of his heart. Every shard, every sliver and scrap, gathered up by her faith in him to create something new. Something whole. And no, just╔ some╔ words╔ completely inappropriate╔ honestly gross╔ disturbing╔Ë He trailed off in horror, unable to articulate his disgust properly. Harryıs chuckles followed him out of the office. Apparently Ron must now accept Draco sodding Malfoy into their friend group. Chapter End Notes A/N: Hello, me again, using my author note to just heap thanks upon all you fantastic readers. We all like Oliver.Ë ÊAgain, your ability to insult a guest without even trying is truly astounding.Ë Ron shrugged, mouth bulging with brussel sprouts. He at least swallowed his food before speaking again. ÊWell consider it my peace offering and Christmas gift to you that I stopped Mum from knitting a ferret onto your sweater. George told her it was your favorite animal. Oh, and Andromedaıs in the kitchen, wants a word with you.Ë He casually dropped that information on Draco as if it didnıt have the power to upend Dracoıs entire world. ÊTactful as ever,Ë Draco grumbled and straightened his lapels as he stood. Draco caught Potterıs eye as he weaved his way through the crowded sitting room. A young boy in a scarlet sweater with matching hair sat at his side, chattering at a rapid pace about the new broom he hoped to receive for Christmas. He owed his aunt the first of these awkward reunions. Draco harbored eternal gratitude at meeting his aunt in the Weasley home and not accidentally happening upon her in a public setting. Her resemblance to Bellatrix startled him at first, but then the older witch smiled, an expression rooted in genuine joy instead of feral madness, and it transformed her features into those of an entirely separate person. Andromeda had hair in a softer brown than her sister, her waves neat with streaks of light gray as opposed to the gleaming dark curls of Bellatrix. It was in the rigidity yet elegance of her posture, and Draco knew that when she spoke, her cadence would be crisp from a childhoodıs worth of elocution lessons. ÊYou do favor your father, it is a rather extraordinary likeness,Ë her keen eyes narrowed as they swept over his face. ÊBut there is some Black in you. The set of your brow is completely Narcissa, as is the shape of your eyes.Ë ÊI inherited her hands as well, Iım told,Ë he offered. ÊHad I practiced more, I may have rivaled her skills on the piano.Ë Andromeda looked briefly touched. ÊShe kept it? The Bluthner?Ë Draco nodded. ÊI took lessons on my birth gift my entire childhood. My thank you for such a grand gift is long overdue, I fear.Ë Her eyes tightened. ÊYes, well, that is hardly your fault. It pleases me to know it was used as intended.Ë An awkward silence filled the air, Draco feeling the weight of literal decades of missed bonding between them. This felt so wrong, that his first introduction to his aunt should take place in a home owned by neither of their families. Is the gulf too wide? Are the wounds too deep? ÊI╔Ë Draco began uncomfortably and tugged at his collar. ÊIım sorry.Ë Andromeda pursed her lips in confusion and it was so similar to his own mother that his heart clenched. ÊWhatever for dear boy?Ë Draco swallowed the anxious bubble of nervousness and grief that suddenly lodged in his throat. ÊFor everything,Ë he replied hoarsely. ÊThat we have to meet like this. That I never knew your daughter or your husband. That╔ that they died because ofÐË ÊPlease,Ë she held up a hand. ÊBut I didnıt want to intrude on your life. I did not want you to feel as if you were under any obligation to meet me.Ë ÊI would have welcomed it,Ë Draco replied seriously. Thank you for thinking of me.Ë Andromeda waved her gratitude away with a delicate hand. ÊThank you for the poetry books! Iım delighted to add to my collection. ÊJust you wait, darling. Next year Iım gifting you a matching set.Ë Next year. ÊAnd I will be burning both sets.Ë She giggled again then yawned. He only knew contentment. December 28, 2008 Hermione sighed and swirled her Bordeaux gently around her glass. At both formal and family events, she kept herself to a strict two-drink maximum rule, but Merlin, her parentsı party tested her resolve. A few years ago, the Grangers reinstated their traditional colleagues/neighbors/friends holiday get-together hosted in their home. At least this year, she had Draco by her side to suffer jointly. The fitting is positively flawless.Ë) Perhaps because every other guest seemed so taken with him, the only expression her mother could manage was pursed lips while her father couldnıt seem to stop openly scowling. Draco leaned against the counter and ran a hand through his hair. ÊIım sorry,Ë she exhaled after a long sip. ÊAs unbearable as this is for me, Iım sure youıd kill for a Disillusionment Charm about now.Ë ÊOh I donıt know,Ë he drawled. Sheıd learned well the unpredictability of life, how the choices one made created such complex outcomes that the art of seeing into the future was laughable to her. A poised statue, emotions occluded and closed off, layers of protection built over the real Draco. When he finally spoke his tone was low and careful. ÊAre you asking me if I see an end date to being with you?Ë She nodded, unable to trust her voice. ÊNo.Ë His voice was firm, his eyes flashing steel. ÊNo, Hermione. ÊEvery version of my future includes you. ÊThat suits me just fine since I have no intention of ever letting you go,Ë she whispered before taking possession of his lips in a burning kiss. While it may not have been a declaration of forever or marriage or bonds or always, it felt momentous enough to Hermione to warrant the giddiness taking hold of her. The joy bloomed outward and stole over her features, and she smiled so hard against his lips it soon became difficult for him to actually kiss her. Draco cupped her face and pulled back. His grin unfurled lazily, entrancing Hermione with the deliberate way his mouth curved upward and then widened, his eyes crinkling at the corners. A happiness that reflected her own. ÊI love you,Ë he said and Hermione welcomed the warm, comforting weight of his body over hers as he gently laid her head back onto her pillow. He made slow and purposeful love to her, and repeated those three words over and over until Hermione couldnıt tell if her body flew or fell. She could only breathlessly return the words, whimper his name, and clutch at his skin in her endeavor to possess every part of him he could give to her. Draco gasped her name into the side of her neck when he came, mere seconds after sheıd done the same for him. I want you every day for the rest of my life. Chapter End Notes A/N: Weıre into the final ten chapters and I donıt know how to feel about that. Any other man would have been uncomfortable, would have looked odd and unnatural buried under layers of silk, black velvet, gold brocade, and numerous brass buttons, but not Draco. No, this man wore an honest-to-gods cape around his shoulders and somehow managed to appear in his natural element, as if he were born to be clothed in multiple layers of extraneous formality. Born with a goblin-made spoon in his mouth. Hermione thought she looked ridiculous, but Draco told her she looked beautiful in her formal robes. Draco also told her she looked beautiful that morning in her fleece pajamas with holes in the elbows, so perhaps he held a slightly skewed perspective. Dracoıs confidence in her helped steel her for the night ahead to be sure, but Hermione took no chances and, determined to not make a fool of herself at Narcissaıs New Yearıs Eve Ball, enlisted the assistance of Padma. As studious in nature as Hermione at times, Padma schooled her in all the intricacies of the unspoken rules and regulations of comporting oneself at a society event hosted by pureblood royalty. They arrived exactly on time, much to Dracoıs annoyance as he would have preferred to be fashionably late, but Hermione wanted the meet and greet portion with Narcissa over with as quickly as possible. A short receiving line had already formed at the door to the ballroom, but she and Draco moved right to the front of the queue. Hermione swallowed nervously and allowed Padmaıs calm voice to echo in her mind, acting as her guide into this world of unmatched wealth and prestige. You always greet the host family first, but since youıre arriving with Draco, you two will obviously skip to the front of the receiving line to Narcissa. She will offer both sides of her face for you to kiss. Narcissa looked regal as ever in robes of a similar design to Hermioneıs, though the color was a sparkling icy blue. Ice like her heart, Hermione thought bitterly, and quickly chastised herself. Pettiness would get her nowhere. Narcissa greeted her son warmly and offered her face to Hermione, who performed the polite social ritual per Padmaıs instruction. She eyed Hermione up and down appraisingly before her cool stare stopped dead on Hermioneıs neck. ÊDid Draco give you that necklace?Ë ÊOh ah, no.Ë Hermione didnıt want to elaborate and make this moment any more awkward than it already was, but Narcissa glared at her throat like a woman possessed. ÊAndromeda lent it to me for the evening.Ë Narcissaıs eyes flashed up to hers at the mention of the disowned Black sister, startling Hermione with the depth of emotion displayed there. ÊIt belonged to my mother,Ë she divulged evenly, and Hermione couldn't decipher whether the woman was furious, shocked, sad, or perhaps a confusing mix of all three. Draco, probably sensing the dangerous turn in the atmosphere swiftly moved Hermione along into the ballroom while she stewed in a combination of both exasperation at Andromedaıs power play and amusement at the audacity of it. That woman would be getting quite the curious owl from Hermione in the next week. When they were a safe distance away, Draco chuckled under his breath. ÊI see Aunt Andromeda never lost her familyıs propensity for causing drama.Ë Hermione huffed, but couldnıt deny her respect for the older witch. ÊYes, I can certainly see how she may have influenced Sirius at any rate. I do wish she would have informed me of her true intentions with this necklace.Ë ÊIt certainly becomes you,Ë Draco intoned, catching her in a heated stare. He plucked two glasses of champagne from a passing tray and handed one to her. She accepted the beverage with a demure smile, Padmaıs instruction in her mind once more. Ladies are expected to stick to champagne and white wine only. Red wine has too high a risk for spills and stains to the teeth, obviously. Hard liquor is considered too masculine and butterbeer too common. While Hermione enjoyed champagne just fine, especially on the occasion of New Yearıs Eve, she certainly didnıt plan to imbibe much tonight. Sheıd need her wits about her with this crowd. She sipped her drink and cast her gaze around the grand room, successful in repressing her snort lest Draco think she meant to mock him or his family. Grandiose did not even begin to describe the ballroom of the Lestrange Estate. The floors beneath her feet gleamed so brightly, she could almost see her own reflection in them. Light sparkled all around and above them, bouncing and refracting throughout the space in the hundreds of crystal pieces that made up the countless chandeliers floating above, not to mention all the cumbersome jewelry glittering around the necks of every woman here. Champagne towers glistened in every corner of the ballroom, the bubbly liquid cascading down in an endless stream of luxury. Airborne silver platters passed by and through the guests, weighed down with all manner of decadent hors dıoeuvres, and though Hermione made sure to eat prior to the gala, (Avoid all finger food. Youıll have to save your public eating for the seated, plated meal, Iım afraid, advised Padma) her mouth watered at the tray of smoked salmon cruditÄs. ÊShall we go find Theo?Ë Dracoıs question broke into her inner musings about the unfair expectations placed upon women at all levels of society and she nodded, eager for some friendly company. Theo nursed a glass of amber liquid with a pained smile on his face and Astoria Greengrass on his arm, sporting an equally tight smile. Weıll be retiring there together after the fireworks later. Iım so looking forward to the spectacle!Ë ÊEnjoy your evening Lady Pliska, Baron,Ë said Draco, and though itıs a perfectly polite dismissal, to the practiced ear his tone was the aural equivalent of the two-finger salute. Obligatory socializing complete for the time being, Hermione was all too glad for the dancing to begin, even if it required her to be the center of attention again. Since Narcissa is widowed and not remarried, Draco and you will be expected to open the dancing for the evening. Donıt be surprised if immediately following, you are approached by other wizards to ask for a turn. You two arenıt betrothed or married, so he has no official claim over you for the dancing. When Draco placed a hand on her waist, she felt that familiar thrill at his touch, even if far too much fabric separated her skin and his fingertips. He gave her hand one reassuring squeeze before waltzing them deftly about the room, his silver eyes on her the entire time. As they turned and glided across the floor, Hermioneıs eye drifted to two enormous silk banners hung along one wall. They depicted the two families Narcissa represented and the two bloodlines that ran through Draco. The Black family crest bore the same motto Hermione remembered from the tapestry hung in Grimmauld Place: Toujours Pur. Hung by its side, the Malfoy familyıs boldly stated Sanctimonia Vincet Semper. Purity Always Conquers. It does not, Hermione thought fiercely. It most certainly does not. Draco had a running list in his head of every guest who, come the New Year, would suddenly find themselves without the usual financial support of House Malfoy. Every open sneer, every furious glare, every heated whisper, every wipe of a hand against fabric after shaking Hermioneıs hand, none of it went unnoticed by Draco. ÊI should probably find Astoria again, have either of you seen her?Ë ÊI havenıt, but perhaps sheıs in the ladiesı? I know I need to find one, Iıll let you know if sheıs in there,Ë suggested Hermione and they escorted her from the hall to the washroom. Waiting for Hermione to return, hopefully with Astoria in tow, the silence of the empty corridor was shattered by what sounded like yelling and the slamming of a nearby door. Theo and Draco exchanged a panicked look, then drew their wands and followed the source of the commotion around a corner. Muffled shouting came from behind the oak doors to one of the traveling parlours. When Theo and Draco burst into the room, they were greeted with quite the startling sight. ÊÐshould be MINE! ThisÐthisÐwholeÐbloodyÐlife! It was always meant to be m-me!Ë Draco had never seen Pansy drunk before. Dirty little mudblood!Ë Draco whirled around to see Hermione frozen at the threshold. She seemed shocked rather than offended, but all Draco wanted to do in that moment was gather her up in his arms and protect her from ever hearing that slur ever again. He heard Theoıs soothing murmur as he swept Hermione away. Pansy continued her unhinged rant. ÊMyÐmy childrenÐshould be yours!Ë she regarded Draco with bulging, mad eyes. ÊIt was always supposedÐsupposed to be me! Me on your arm! Your heirs in my womb! Not that beastly littleÐË ÊInsult Granger again in front of me and Iıll hex you through that fireplace,Ë he interrupted coldly. ÊDraco, leave it, sheıs not well right now,Ë said Astoria firmly. ÊAdrian, please, take her to our home, our elves can take care of her.Ë Adrian and Daphne finally succeeded in dragging Pansy through the Floo, leaving an ominous silence in her shrieking wake. ÊYou shouldnıt have needled her, sheıs suffered enough,Ë said Astoria. Draco fixed her with an angry stare. ÊSuffered? Sheıs the one who chose to drink tonight! Sheıs the one who leapt into a marriage for status and wealth! Pansy made her bed,Ë he retorted. ÊOh? And what were her other options? What options exist for a sole pureblood heiress under the thumb of her overbearing parents?Ë she argued back. ÊBit rich coming from you, a Greengrass. Your family is hardly the rebellious type. And Iım meant to feel sorry for poor, spoiled Pansy? Sheıs reaping what sheıs sown.Ë Astoria quickly replaced her smile with a cold, incredulous glare. ÊYou think she deserves to be treated in such a way by her own husband? The man is a well-known philanderer and on top of that╔ well you heard Pansy didnıt you? The poor woman has multiple children with a man she does not love. Do you think her husband allowed her a say in that decision? You think any witch deserves such a life? As much as he cringed thinking back upon his time with Pansy, Astoria had a point. ÊI know you have a difficult history with Pansy,Ë continued Astoria gently. ÊBut youıll never be in that position. At Hogwarts, Draco needed to seek the approval of the older Slytherin, given his position as captain of the quidditch team, but found the boy rather distasteful. ÊYouıve no idea╔ before I╔ well, before all of╔ this, Iıd honestly given up hope, and was willing to bend to my parentsı wishes. I resigned myself to the life of pureblood wife. Indeed, he looked so forlorn that Hermione looped an arm through his and leant her head against his shoulder. ÊI hate this,Ë he continued, his voice as soft and measured as always in that particular ÊTheoË way of speaking. Often Hermione had wondered what drew Draco and Theo together as friends besides their shared heritage. One of a withdrawn child, precise in his movements, keeping himself constantly to the edges of every conversation and every room he entered. Iıll help her any way I can, sheıs truly wonderful Theo.Ë He grinned down at her. Just one positive comment about his girlfriend swung his mood right back around. ÊShe is. She really is. Are you ready to return to battle?Ë Hermione sighed and slid away from him. ÊAs ready as Iıll ever be I suppose.Ë She straightened up and worried her lower lip between her teeth. I donıt care if weıre in the Muggle cafÄ or a grand ballroom, you belong with me,Ë he intoned. ÊI love you,Ë she replied, the only words available to her in the moment. Iıd lavish you with thousands of jewels if you let me, love.Ë Hermione rolled her eyes, not doubting for a second that Draco would absolutely follow through on that threat if she would only relent. Midnight edged closer and Draco and Hermione rode out the rest of the evening in each otherıs arms on the dance floor. When the orchestra conductor announced that the New Year was but 10 seconds away, Draco gathered her close, his intent to snog her thoroughly spelled out in the gleam of his eyes. As the countdown hit Êone!Ë the entire ballroom erupted in cheers and Draco leaned down to press a sweet, lingering kiss to her lips. He pulled back with a grin, but Hermioneıs eye caught once again on the banner behind his head. Purity Always Conquers. She redirected her gaze to Draco, and with a sudden surge of what felt like triumph in the face of centuries of meaningless bigotry and hatred, captured his willing lips in a second kiss. Bollocks to your purity. Love conquers all. Chapter End Notes A/N: Thank you for reading! And thank you for all the wonderful messages and comments, itıs helping me cope as we count down to the end of this story. Draco gave his lower limbs an irritable shake, dislodging the obstinate creature who had rendered his legs partially numb. The ginger furball gave him a mutinous glare before flouncing off the bed. Hermioneıs comment to Pansy last night about retiring to private quarters within the Lestrange Estate was all bluster. Theyıd instead exchanged stilted goodbyes with Narcissa and immediately left for Hermioneıs home where she made good on her promise to let him shag her in naught but a goblin-wrought necklace. She might try to downplay it, but Draco thought his girlfriend looked ever so enticing when nude and riding him while draped in expensive jewels. Said girlfriend stirred sleepily at his side then pressed a kiss to his shoulder. ÊIıll fix the eggs and bacon, but youıre responsible for your own coffee. I believe in you, I think you can finally master the brewing machine,Ë she teased and Draco rolled his eyes. Now quite at home in Hermioneıs kitchen he was more pleased than he intended to ever admit that he successfully made a pot of coffee all by himself. He picked up the morning copy of The Daily Prophet and flicked through it idly as Hermione approached from behind and slid her arms around his torso. ÊWill your mother be pleased with the coverage in the society pages this morning?Ë she inquired and bustled past him to fix her tea. Draco took a seat at the kitchen island and flipped toward the back sections of the paper. But a several page spread and dozens of column inches detailing the ins and outs of the most infamous of pureblood holiday balls did not await him on the society pages. No, instead Draco found the press space usually dedicated to Narcissa Malfoy and her gala taken up by an announcement and accompanying story that made his face crack into a wide and incredulous grin. The bubble of loud laughter that burst past his lips caused Hermione to jump and whip around, spatula in hand. ÊAstoria Greengrass, you sneaky, magnificent witch,Ë he chuckled and handed the paper to a confused Hermione. ÊOh my gods╔Ë she whispered in shocked awe and had to take a seat next to Draco. A black and white photograph of Astoria Greengrass and her brand new husband, Dennis Creevey, smiled up at them. Their arms were looped through one anotherıs as an older couple beamed fondly in the background. The paperıs caption identified them as Dennisıs father and step.mother. ÊThey eloped╔ how wonderful!Ë Hermione enthused and eagerly read the details. Apparently, Astoria Greengrass had in truth shown up on the arm of Theo at Narcissaıs party already officially Astoria Creevey. Theyıd been wed at a registrarıs office in Muggle London at noon, with Dennis filing the paperwork to the Ministry of Magic mere minutes before the Hall of Records had closed for the day. Several things fell into place for Draco as he thought back to his conversations with Astoria and he wondered how long the witch had needed to hide her relationship with a Muggleborn. A feeling of pride at her bravery swelled within him. Astoria knew what this would cost her and sheıd pursued her own path regardless. Sheıd probably be disowned, and would have had to plan her exit from her estate for months. ÊWe should send them a wedding present! Iım sure thereıs some old Malfoy doctrine decreeing the provision of heirs with white.blond hair by a certain age for him.Ë ÊOdd that you feel itıs your place to broach the subject at all,Ë chimed in Ginny with a frosty glare at Parvati. But the other woman remained unbothered in her blatant nosiness. ÊPureblood men like him donıt often casually date for so long. Thereıs usually a betrothal contract by now. Wonder what his mother has to say about that.Ë Hermione bristled at the way sheıd reduced her relationship with Draco to Êcasual.Ë ÊYes well, weıre a somewhat unconventional couple and heıs rather uninterested in following those outdated customs,Ë said Hermione evenly. ÊI just find it interesting is all,Ë sniffed Parvati. ÊHe seems the type to demand those customs be followed.Ë ÊThatıs enough Parvati,Ë snapped Padma. ÊDracoıs my friend too. Now stop pestering Hermione and help Anjana with the bustle, Iıll need your help before the reception.Ë Ginny shot Hermione a look that quite clearly asked, ÊWould you like me to hex her for you?Ë But Hermione only repressed a snort and shook her head. Sheıd handled worse comments about her relationship with Draco and she knew thereıd be plenty more for as long as they were together. ÊHowıs your research paper coming along?Ë chirped Ginny, changing the subject. ÊAnd did Paulo send you any more of that wine?Ë The distraction tactic deployed by her friend worked, and Hermione talked enthusiastically about her recent collaborative effort with the celebrated Mermish scholar sheıd met in Venice, Paulo Pescaro. Ginny did her the courtesy of listening attentively to the central points of their joint paper based on Hermioneıs previous presentation building on Pauloıs decades of discoveries on the ancient mermaid colonies in the Mediterranean. It was an extracurricular activity outside of her day job at the Ministry, but to have this published in a peer-reviewed journal could potentially do wonders for Hermione if she ever wanted to secure funding for any future private research. Hermione then rewarded her patient friend by divulging that yes, her kind and insightful co-author had in fact shipped her several bottles of Italian wine from one of his sonıs vineyards. Ginny promptly invited her and Draco over for dinner the following weekend. Hermione soldiered through the rest of the afternoon, even delightedly accepting a business card from Anjana with a whispered, Êowl me, please, for any formal events on your social calendar,Ë before she Flooed home. With Astoria and Dennis officially hired and the young married couple off and running with their agreed-upon ideas for her Hogwarts fund, Hermione now had more time on her hands to pursue her varied passion projects, social commitments, and Ministry workload. Sheıd intended to respond to Pauloıs most recent correspondence, as she was left to her own devices for the evening, but concentration failed to ensnare her. Draco was working tonight; attending the Wasps match against the Falmouth Falcons (his favorite match of the season, as heıd grown up a supporter of the Falcons), and without him or her work to distract her, Hermione couldnıt help but dwell on the impertinent conversation with Parvati. She and Draco didnıt owe anyone an explanation for the progression of their relationship; Hermione was secure enough in their affection for each other. But Parvatiıs harsh view of Draco, while misinformed and a little cruel, unfortunately conjured several questions in Hermioneıs mind. Did Draco want to marry her? Did he want to have a child or children with her? She remembered well that night in December after the mortifying debacle that was her parentsı holiday party. ÊEvery version of my future includes you. Iım yours in whatever capacity youıll have me.Ë While yes, that memory of his words caused a happy sigh to leave her lips, what did it actually mean for their future? Would they just continue on as they were, indefinitely? Happy and in love, to be sure, but in separate homes? Sheıd once reached this point long ago with Ron, except theyıd actually taken the step of moving in together. But every time theyıd discussed marriage or children, one of them would demur and suggest punting the discussion to a future date. Hermione would always feel a knot of tension release in her chest whenever they would, once again, successfully avoid speaking seriously about committing to an engagement. But with Draco╔ Heıd become the comfort and familiarity that she sought in relationships while maintaining a spark of excitement and passion that kept her craving his presence in her life. The difference between the Draco sheıd befriended at the cafÄ and the Draco she currently waited on to arrive back from a long night of work was night and day. Heıd stomped (literally) back into her life a closed off, self-loathing man treading water; one who didnıt think he deserved love or happiness. Draco had emotionally developed leaps and bounds beyond that melancholic persona over the course of their friendship and subsequent relationship. Earning his trust and ultimately his love and showing him that yes, you can be vulnerable and still be worthy of these good things in life was a privilege. And heıd had a profound effect on her, too. Sheıd also been content to glide through life, stalled in her career, languishing in the familiar comfort of old friendships, uninspired to put herself out there without a concrete direction. Draco reminded her of all the ambitions sheıd cloaked behind layers of casual acceptance of the world around her. No one was really interested in Mermish runes so why bother? No one would ever support house elf freedom so why bother? A Hogwarts introductory curriculum for Muggleborn children would never be instituted, so why bother? You should bother, heıd essentially said. You care about this, so either make it happen or put your all into at least attempting to make it happen. He challenged her ideas, because gods that man loved an argument with her, and because he knew she could rise to the challenge. His unwavering and honest support of her ambitions and ideals, though at times perhaps at odds with his own personal world view, was everything sheıd ever imagined in a life partner. This was all before even mentioning the sex. Good lord, she could write several tomes-worth on how fantastic the sex was with him and it wouldnıt be long enough to describe all the ways Draco made her feel adored and empowered in the bedroom. Hermione closed her eyes and told herself to take a step back, to filter out all the societal expectations on women her age, all the familial expectations from Dracoıs mother, and all the invasively rude assumptions from everyone else. What did she want? In her heart of hearts, what did forever look like with Draco? When Hermione opened her eyes, her fireplace lit up green and the man himself stepped out of the flames. Draco looked tired; his eyes strained from peering through omnioculars for hours on end, but the second he spotted her on the couch his whole face brightened. ÊHello,Ë he approached and pecked her lips. ÊThe Falcons won and I had to pretend to be upset.Ë He gestured her near empty mug of tea. ÊI could do with some of that, any left?Ë Hermione shook her head and moved to get up. ÊNo, I just made one for myself, let me go andÐË But Draco waved her off and went through to the kitchen. ÊIıve got it, love. If I put on a full kettle will you take some more?Ë Hermione sank back down into the cushions. ÊYes,Ë she replied, dazedly. When he later returned with a mug of his own and a fresh one for her, he kissed the top of her head and settled down next to her. ÊHow was Padmaıs robe fitting?Ë he asked her. Hermione grinned and gave him a brief re-telling of her afternoon with the rest of the bridal party all the while a joyous thought ran in a continuous stream through her mind. I think forever could look like this. Chapter End Notes A/N: It was recently brought to my attention that another person had been posting Remain Nameless on Wattpad without my permission. Hermione couldnıt say, nor could she have properly prepared herself for the sight before her. She stood on the front steps of Hogwarts, looking down upon the vast grounds, and everywhere she looked she saw Muggle parents and family members and young, eager magical children. Down by Hagridıs hut, she saw Rolf Scamander introducing a group of children to nifflers and crups while Hagrid himself showed off a unicorn in the paddock. Out over the quidditch pitch, Hermione spied an airborne Maureen Tyler as she gave a flying demonstration with a few of her Harpies teammates. Dennisıs father, Mitchell, bustled past her with a group of Muggle parents, beginning the tour of the first floor of the castle. Dennis was sat down on the shore of the Black Lake, a casual and inviting atmosphere with blankets on the grass as he entertained the non-magical siblings and answered their questions, encouraging the children to toss bits of bread to the giant squid lurking beneath the surface. Behind her, inside the entrance hall, Hermione heard Astoria and Dean Thomas talking logistics for the luncheon to be served inside the Great Hall. Theyıd poached yet another disillusioned Ministry worker away from the government in Dean, whoıd jumped at the chance to become the volunteer coordinator for the fund. Heıd already pitched several wonderful ideas to attract more volunteers, like offering extra credit to Hogwarts students for Muggle Studies (soon to be renamed Understanding Non-Magical People) and an internship program for Seventh-Years. This was real. Hermione swallowed a lump in her throat and promised herself that she would invite her parents to attend the orientation next year. She knew theyıd have loved something like this and perhaps it would help build further understanding between the three of them about what Hermione wanted to accomplish as a witch. Sheıd shed enough tears this morning when a huge bouquet of flowers arrived from Harry and Ginny. Harry himself penned the card, writing that this program would have made all the difference to a scruffy-looking orphan from Little Whinging with bad hair and taped glasses. Draco vowed to hex Harry and his stupid glasses for making Hermione cry the next time they all met up. Ron and Padma had sent champagne and Ronıs note brought more tears, but this time from laughter, when she read: ÊStill overachieving arenıt you Hermione? Iım sure your program is great and all, but if it doesnıt include locking these kids in a girlsı bathroom with a fully grown mountain troll to force a few friendships, then I think youıve still got some work to do.Ë Seeing as the staff had everything in hand and thanking all the deities for the resourcefulness and ambitions of Astoria, Hermione decided to find Draco. She knew just where he would wander to, and was therefore unsurprised to find him seated with Wesley Macnair in the quidditch stands, observing the awed little ones on the pitch. Draco gestured vaguely to the stands across the way where another man sat, looking delightedly up at Maureen as she skillfully flew around. Even from a considerable distance, there was no mistaking that he was tall and broadly built with neat lines of well-defined muscles. ÊDo you know that man over there?Ë Draco asked Wesley as Hermione joined them. ÊAye, thatıs Moıs father. Heıs a real decent bloke, loves watching her fly.Ë Dracoıs brow furrowed. ÊHeıs a Muggle, right? What does he do for a living?Ë ÊLenıs a weight-lifting coach. Mo grew up going to the gym every day from when she was just a wee thing. How do you think she got into such good shape? That man knows his strength-training.Ë ÊIıll say,Ë Draco muttered and Hermione saw an idea forming behind his eyes as he lapsed into silence. ÊWell Iıd better go find the wife,Ë said Wesley as he stood. ÊSheıll be wanting to have you two over for dinner soon, I expect.Ë ÊOh how lovely! Please tell Lara thank you for all her assistance with scheduling the Hogwarts Express trip!Ë Hermione said in parting and Wesley saluted her. Hermione contented herself with watching the excitement of the soon-to-be Hogwartsı students on the grass below, most faces wide-eyed in wonder at the thought of one day being able to actually fly. ÊIf I were a team owner, Iıd hire that man straight off,Ë Draco piped up suddenly, and Hermione saw he still looked across the way at Mr. Tyler. ÊEven though heıs a Muggle?Ë Draco shrugged. ÊAn expert is an expert. Imagine an entire squad trained by him╔Ë Draco didnıt often talk about the complexities of his job or quidditch with Hermione, despite her insistence that she wanted to hear about his work. ÊIs that something youıd like to do one day? Own a team?Ë she asked, latching on to what heıd revealed in his statement about Maureenıs father. To her surprise, a flush rose to his cheeks. ÊI mean╔ yes, one day. Scouting is nice and all, but since my dream of being a professional player was never realized╔ yeah. Iıve given a lot of thought to one day being an owner.Ë ÊWhatıs stopping you?Ë Draco snorted. ÊCertainly nothing on the financial front. But no owners have been eager to sell in the last decade, really. Although there are rumors the Falmouth Falcons owner is looking to retire from ownership and none of his children want to take on the team. But still, theyıd have to be willing to sell to me╔ but it doesnıt matter anyway. Itıs just a silly childhood dream of mine.Ë Hermione stared at him pensively. The occurrences were fewer these days, but she sometimes still detected that Draco held back from fully pursuing things he truly wanted in life, as if he still felt he needed to be punished. ÊI think you should have a chat with Mr. Tyler. Couldnıt hurt right?Ë Draco shrugged but Hermione pressed on. ÊOh, go on, have Wesley introduce you. Iıve got a couple more groups to observe so youıll be left to your own devices anyway.Ë She pecked his cheek and wandered off to check in with more of the volunteers, hoping Draco would take her suggestion to heart. Hours later, Hermione waved goodbye to their first ever group of visiting families, as the Muggle family members were escorted down to stay in Hogsmeade and the incoming students being shown to the dormitories. This part of the introduction to the magical world had been an unmitigated success and she hoped the tours of Hogsmeade and Diagon Alley would prove just as fruitful. Draco caught up with Hermione just outside the Entrance Hall, having just concluded a lengthy chat with Mr. Tyler. ÊI gave Len my card. Weıll see what happens I suppose.Ë Hermione shot him a grin. ÊOn first name basis already are we?Ë Draco playfully nudged her shoulder with his own. ÊYes, yes, you continue to be right about all things.Ë The easy smile on his face warmed her heart and seized her with a sudden surge of affection. She reached down and intertwined their fingers. ÊI donıt quite know how to thank you for all this╔Ë Hermione said quietly. ÊItıs everything and more╔ itıs exceeded my wildest expectations and I╔ I just╔Ë She took a steadying breath and held his gaze. ÊThank you.Ë Draco shook his head and gave her a small smile. ÊNo love, this is all down to you. Your vision, your desire to see something this comprehensive become reality. ÊI love you╔ so much╔Ë She tugged him forward then dragged his head down to capture his lips. Draco responded eagerly, and they soon found themselves in the exact same position from the inaugural gala the year before: pressed against the stone wall and blissfully unaware of their surroundings and snogging each other senseless. Indeed, they were once again so unaware that neither noticed Minerva McGonagall pass by with a scoff and a muttered, ÊYouıd think the novelty would have worn off by now╔Ë December 2009 Christmas shopping in Diagon Alley or Hogsmeade would not be in the cards for Hermione this year and sheıd do well to accept that fact. While happy for Ron and Padma and though their wedding in India may have been her favorite (donıt tell Ginny), the subsequent media circus made it quite impossible to be seen anywhere in wizarding public for the time being. If the commotion and press attention, positive though it may be for now, didnıt abate soon, then everyone in her life would either receive her horrid attempts at knitting or sheıd have to brave the hustle and bustle of the Muggle shopping world near holiday time. Although, owl-order catalogues could help, especially if it meant she could pick out magical gifts without hearing, ÊMiss Granger, when do you and Mr. Malfoy plan to makes things official?Ë shouted at her or have strangers shove photographs of her with Draco from the recent crop of pictures from Ron and Padmaıs extravagant nuptials under her nose for her autograph. Part of her felt slightly bitter that Ron and Padma had embarked on a month-long honeymoon to Japan immediately following the wedding and therefore did not have to suffer the press attention that the rest of their family and friends now endured. ÊWell of course not, he only exists to serve the household and we wouldnıt want to give elves ideas above their station,Ë she tossed out bitterly. ÊI am unable to call Watson.Ë A pop signaled the return of Crick, this time with a second elf.
